## Aptitude — Standard 10

**Q1. David gets on the elevator at the 11 th floor of a building and rides up at the rate of 57 floors per minute. At the same time, Albert gets on an elevator at the 51st floor of the same building and rides down at the rate of 63 floors per minute. If they continue travelling at these rates, then at which floor will their paths cross ?**

- **A.** 19
- **B.** 28
- **C.** 30  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 37

**Explanation:** Suppose their paths cross after x minutes. Then, 11 + 57 x = 51 - 63 x 120 x = 40 x = 1 3 Number of floors covered by David in (1/3) min. = 1 x 57 = 19. 3 So, their paths cross at (11 +19) i.e., 30 th floor.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003)*

---

**Q2. The price of 2 sarees and 4 shirts is Rs. 1600. With the same money one can buy 1 saree and 6 shirts. If one wants to buy 12 shirts, how much shall he have to pay ?**

- **A.** Rs. 1200
- **B.** Rs. 2400  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Rs. 4800
- **D.** Cannot be determined

**Explanation:** Let the price of a saree and a shirt be Rs. x and Rs. y respectively. Then, 2 x + 4 y = 1600 .... (i) and x + 6 y = 1600 .... (ii) Divide equation (i) by 2, we get the below equation. => x + 2y = 800. --- (iii) Now subtract (iii) from (ii) x + 6y = 1600 (-) x + 2y = 800 ---------------- 4y = 800 ---------------- Therefore, y = 200. Now apply value of y in (iii) => x + 2 x 200 = 800 => x + 400 = 800 Therefore x = 400 Solving (i) and (ii) we get x = 400, y = 200. Cost of 12 shirts = Rs. (12 x 200) = Rs. 2400.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/)*

---

**Q3. The average weight of 8 person's increases by 2.5 kg when a new person comes in place of one of them weighing 65 kg. What might be the weight of the new person?**

- **A.** 76 kg
- **B.** 76.5 kg
- **C.** 85 kg  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Data inadequate

**Explanation:** Total weight increased = (8 x 2.5) kg = 20 kg. Weight of new person = (65 + 20) kg = 85 kg. Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/ceg2jvHsiJU

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/)*

---

**Q4. A pupil's marks were wrongly entered as 83 instead of 63. Due to that the average marks for the class got increased by half (1/2). The number of pupils in the class is:**

- **A.** 10
- **B.** 20
- **C.** 40  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 73

**Explanation:** Let there be x pupils in the class. Total increase in marks = x x 1 = x 2 2 x = (83 - 63) x = 20 x = 40. 2 2

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003)*

---

**Q5. In Arun's opinion, his weight is greater than 65 kg but less than 72 kg. His brother does not agree with Arun and he thinks that Arun's weight is greater than 60 kg but less than 70 kg. His mother's view is that his weight cannot be greater than 68 kg. If all are them are correct in their estimation, what is the average of different probable weights of Arun?**

- **A.** 67 kg.  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 68 kg.
- **C.** 69 kg.
- **D.** Data inadequate

**Explanation:** Let Arun's weight by X kg. According to Arun, 65 < X < 72 According to Arun's brother, 60 < X < 70. According to Arun's mother, X <= 68 The values satisfying all the above conditions are 66, 67 and 68. Required average = 66 + 67 + 68 = 201 = 67 kg. 3 3

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002)*

---

**Q6. To fill a tank, 25 buckets of water is required. How many buckets of water will be required to fill the same tank if the capacity of the bucket is reduced to two-fifth of its present ?**

- **A.** 10
- **B.** 35
- **C.** 62.5  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Cannot be determined

**Explanation:** Let the capacity of 1 bucket = x . Then, the capacity of tank = 25 x . New capacity of bucket = 2 x 5 Required number of buckets = 25 x (2 x /5) = 25 x x 5 2 x = 125 2 = 62.5

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002)*

---

**Q7. In an election between two candidates, one got 55% of the total valid votes, 20% of the votes were invalid. If the total number of votes was 7500, the number of valid votes that the other candidate got, was:**

- **A.** 2700  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 2900
- **C.** 3000
- **D.** 3100

**Explanation:** Number of valid votes = 80% of 7500 = 6000. Valid votes polled by other candidate = 45% of 6000 = 45 x 6000 = 2700. 100

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002)*

---

**Q8. If A = x % of y and B = y % of x , then which of the following is true?**

- **A.** A is smaller than B.  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** A is greater than B
- **C.** Relationship between A and B cannot be determined.
- **D.** If x is smaller than y , then A is greater than B.

**Explanation:** x % of y = x x y = y x x = y % of x 100 100 A = B.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/)*

---

**Q9. The population of a town increased from 1,75,000 to 2,62,500 in a decade. The average percent increase of population per year is:**

- **A.** 4.37%
- **B.** 5%  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 6%
- **D.** 8.75%

**Explanation:** Increase in 10 years = (262500 - 175000) = 87500. Increase% = 87500 x 100 % = 50%. 175000 Required average = 50 % = 5%. 10

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003)*

---

**Q10. In a cricket team, the average age of eleven players in 28 years. What is the age of the captain? I. The captain is eleven years older than the youngest player. II. The average age of 10 players, other than the captain is 27.3 years. III. Leaving aside the captain and the youngest player, the average ages of three groups of three players each are 25 years, 28 years and 30 years respectively.**

- **A.** Any two of the three
- **B.** All I, II and III
- **C.** II only or I and III only  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** II and III only

**Explanation:** Total age of 11 players = (28 x 11) years = 308 years. I. C = Y + 11 C - Y = 11 .... (i) II. Total age of 10 players (excluding captain) = (27.3 x 10) years = 273 years. Age of captain = (308 - 273) years = 35 years. Thus, C = 35. .... (ii) From (i) and (ii), we get Y = 24 III. Total age of 9 players = [ (25 x 3) + (28 x 3) + (30 x 3)] years = 249 years. C + Y = (308 - 249) = 59 .... (iii) From (i) and (iii), we get C = 35. Thus, II alone gives the answer. Also, I and III together give the answer. Correct answer is (C).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/008001](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/008001)*

---

## Aptitude — Standard 6

**Q11. A man has Rs.480 in the denominations of one-rupee notes, five-rupee notes and ten-rupee notes. The number of notes of each denomination is equal. What is the total number of notes that he has ?**

- **A.** 45
- **B.** 60
- **C.** 75
- **D.** 90  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let number of notes of each denomination be x . Then x + 5 x + 10 x = 480 16 x = 480 x = 30. Hence, total number of notes = 3 x = 90.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/)*

---

**Q12. In a regular week, there are 5 working days and for each day, the working hours are 8. A man gets Rs. 2.40 per hour for regular work and Rs. 3.20 per hours for overtime. If he earns Rs. 432 in 4 weeks, then how many hours does he work for ?**

- **A.** 160
- **B.** 175  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 180
- **D.** 195

**Explanation:** Suppose the man works overtime for x hours. Now, working hours in 4 weeks = (5 x 8 x 4) = 160. 160 x 2.40 + x x 3.20 = 432 3.20 x = 432 - 384 = 48 x = 15. Hence, total hours of work = (160 + 15) = 175.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003)*

---

**Q13. The average age of P, Q, R and S is 30 years. How old is R? I. The sum of ages of P and R is 60 years. II. S is 10 years younger than R.**

- **A.** I alone sufficient while II alone not sufficient to answer
- **B.** II alone sufficient while I alone not sufficient to answer
- **C.** Either I or II alone sufficient to answer
- **D.** Both I and II are not sufficient to answer  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** P + Q + R + S = (30 x 4) P + Q + R + S = 120 .... (i) I. P + R = 60 .... (ii) II. S = (R - 10) .... (iii) From (i), (ii) and (iii), we cannot find R. Correct answer is (D)

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001)*

---

**Q14. The average weight of A, B and C is 45 kg. If the average weight of A and B be 40 kg and that of B and C be 43 kg, then the weight of B is:**

- **A.** 17 kg
- **B.** 20 kg
- **C.** 26 kg
- **D.** 31 kg  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let A, B, C represent their respective weights. Then, we have: A + B + C = (45 x 3) = 135 .... (i) A + B = (40 x 2) = 80 .... (ii) B + C = (43 x 2) = 86 ....(iii) Adding (ii) and (iii), we get: A + 2B + C = 166 .... (iv) Subtracting (i) from (iv), we get : B = 31. B's weight = 31 kg.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003)*

---

**Q15. In the first 10 overs of a cricket game, the run rate was only 3.2. What should be the run rate in the remaining 40 overs to reach the target of 282 runs?**

- **A.** 6.25  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 6.5
- **C.** 6.75
- **D.** 7

**Explanation:** Required run rate = 282 - (3.2 x 10) = 250 = 6.25 40 40 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/GhK9d8tcqvA

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/)*

---

**Q16. Three candidates contested an election and received 1136, 7636 and 11628 votes respectively. What percentage of the total votes did the winning candidate get?**

- **A.** 57%  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 60%
- **C.** 65%
- **D.** 90%

**Explanation:** Total number of votes polled = (1136 + 7636 + 11628) = 20400. Required percentage = 11628 x 100 % = 57%. 20400

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003)*

---

**Q17. The captain of a cricket team of 11 members is 26 years old and the wicket keeper is 3 years older. If the ages of these two are excluded, the average age of the remaining players is one year less than the average age of the whole team. What is the average age of the team?**

- **A.** 23 years  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 24 years
- **C.** 25 years
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** Let the average age of the whole team by x years. 11 x - (26 + 29) = 9( x -1) 11 x - 9 x = 46 2 x = 46 x = 23. So, average age of the team is 23 years.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002)*

---

**Q18. A sum of Rs. 1360 has been divided among A, B and C such that A gets of what B gets and B gets of what C gets. B's share is:**

- **A.** Rs. 120
- **B.** Rs. 160
- **C.** Rs. 240  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Rs. 300

**Explanation:** Let C's share = Rs. x Then, B's share = Rs. x , A's share = Rs. 2 x x = Rs. x 4 3 4 6 x + x + x = 1360 6 4 17 x = 1360 12 x = 1360 x 12 = Rs. 960 17 Hence, B's share = Rs. 960 = Rs. 240. 4

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002)*

---

**Q19. If 20% of a = b , then b % of 20 is the same as:**

- **A.** 4% of a  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 5% of a
- **C.** 20% of a
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** 20% of a = b 20 a = b . 100 b % of 20 = b x 20 = 20 a x 1 x 20 = 4 a = 4% of a . 100 100 100 100

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002)*

---

**Q20. A batsman scored 110 runs which included 3 boundaries and 8 sixes. What percent of his total score did he make by running between the wickets?**

- **A.** 45%
- **B.** 45 5 % 11  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 54 6 % 11
- **D.** 55%

**Explanation:** Number of runs made by running = 110 - (3 x 4 + 8 x 6) = 110 - (60) = 50. Required percentage = 50 x 100 % = 45 5 % 110 11 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/X2zTnABqEHU

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/)*

## Aptitude — Standard 7

**Q21. Two students appeared at an examination. One of them secured 9 marks more than the other and his marks was 56% of the sum of their marks. The marks obtained by them are:**

- **A.** 39, 30
- **B.** 41, 32
- **C.** 42, 33  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 43, 34

**Explanation:** Let their marks be ( x + 9) and x . Then, x + 9 = 56 ( x + 9 + x ) 100 25( x + 9) = 14(2 x + 9) 3 x = 99 x = 33 So, their marks are 42 and 33.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/)*

---

**Q22. The average weight of 16 boys in a class is 50.25 kg and that of the remaining 8 boys is 45.15 kg. Find the average weights of all the boys in the class.**

- **A.** 47.55 kg
- **B.** 48 kg
- **C.** 48.55 kg  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 49.25 kg

**Explanation:** Required average = 50.25 x 16 + 45.15 x 8 16 + 8 = 804 + 361.20 24 = 1165.20 24 = 48.55

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003)*

---

**Q23. A family consists of two grandparents, two parents and three grandchildren. The average age of the grandparents is 67 years, that of the parents is 35 years and that of the grandchildren is 6 years. What is the average age of the family?**

- **A.** 28 4 years 7
- **B.** 31 5 years 7  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 32 1 years 7
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** Required average = 67 x 2 + 35 x 2 + 6 x 3 2 + 2 + 3 = 134 + 70 + 18 7 = 222 7 = 31 5 years. 7 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/OXLnoItd0MA

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/)*

---

**Q24. One-third of Rahul's savings in National Savings Certificate is equal to one-half of his savings in Public Provident Fund. If he has Rs. 1,50,000 as total savings, how much has he saved in Public Provident Fund ?**

- **A.** Rs. 30,000
- **B.** Rs. 50,000
- **C.** Rs. 60,000  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Rs. 90,000

**Explanation:** Let savings in N.S.C and P.P.F. be Rs. x and Rs. (150000 - x ) respectively. Then, 1 x = 1 (150000 - x ) 3 2 x + x = 75000 3 2 5 x = 75000 6 x = 75000 x 6 = 90000 5 Savings in Public Provident Fund = Rs. (150000 - 90000) = Rs. 60000

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002)*

---

**Q25. Two tailors X and Y are paid a total of Rs. 550 per week by their employer. If X is paid 120 percent of the sum paid to Y, how much is Y paid per week?**

- **A.** Rs. 200
- **B.** Rs. 250  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Rs. 300
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** Let the sum paid to Y per week be Rs. z . Then, z + 120% of z = 550. z + 120 z = 550 100 11 z = 550 5 z = 550 x 5 = 250. 11

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003)*

---

**Q26. There are two examinations rooms A and B. If 10 students are sent from A to B, then the number of students in each room is the same. If 20 candidates are sent from B to A, then the number of students in A is double the number of students in B. The number of students in room A is:**

- **A.** 20
- **B.** 80
- **C.** 100  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 200

**Explanation:** Let the number of students in rooms A and B be x and y respectively. Then, x - 10 = y + 10 x - y = 20 .... (i) and x + 20 = 2( y - 20) x - 2 y = -60 .... (ii) Solving (i) and (ii) we get: x = 100 , y = 80. The required answer A = 100.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/)*

---

**Q27. Free notebooks were distributed equally among children of a class. The number of notebooks each child got was one-eighth of the number of children. Had the number of children been half, each child would have got 16 notebooks. Total how many notebooks were distributed ?**

- **A.** 256
- **B.** 432
- **C.** 512  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 640

**Explanation:** Let total number of children be x . Then, x x 1 x = x x 16 x = 64. 8 2 Number of notebooks = 1 x 2 = 1 x 64 x 64 = 512. 8 8

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003)*

---

**Q28. The average monthly income of P and Q is Rs. 5050. The average monthly income of Q and R is Rs. 6250 and the average monthly income of P and R is Rs. 5200. The monthly income of P is:**

- **A.** 3500
- **B.** 4000  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 4050
- **D.** 5000

**Explanation:** Let P, Q and R represent their respective monthly incomes. Then, we have: P + Q = (5050 x 2) = 10100 .... (i) Q + R = (6250 x 2) = 12500 .... (ii) P + R = (5200 x 2) = 10400 .... (iii) Adding (i), (ii) and (iii), we get: 2(P + Q + R) = 33000 or P + Q + R = 16500 .... (iv) Subtracting (ii) from (iv), we get P = 4000. P's monthly income = Rs. 4000.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002)*

---

**Q29. How many candidates were interviewed everyday by the panel A out of the three panels A, B and C? I. The three panels on average interview 15 candidates every day. II. Out of a total of 45 candidates interviewed everyday by the three panels, the number of candidates interviewed by panel A is more by 2 than the candidates interviewed by panel c and is more by 1 than the candidates interviewed by panel B.**

- **A.** I alone sufficient while II alone not sufficient to answer
- **B.** II alone sufficient while I alone not sufficient to answer  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Either I or II alone sufficient to answer
- **D.** Both I and II are not sufficient to answer

**Explanation:** I. Total candidates interviewed by 3 panels = (15 x 3) = 45. II. Let x candidates be interviewed by C. Number of candidates interviewed by A = ( x + 2). Number of candidates interviewed by B = ( x + 1). x + ( x + 2) + ( x + 1) = 45 3 x = 42 x = 14 Hence, the correct answer is (B).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001)*

---

**Q30. In a certain school, 20% of students are below 8 years of age. The number of students above 8 years of age is of the number of students of 8 years of age which is 48. What is the total number of students in the school?**

- **A.** 72  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 80
- **C.** 120
- **D.** 150

**Explanation:** Let the number of students be x . Then, Number of students above 8 years of age = (100 - 20)% of x = 80% of x . 80% of x = 48 + 2 of 48 3 80 x = 80 100 x = 100. Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/yPfocU6DA2M

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002)*

---

## Aptitude — Standard 8

**Q31. Gauri went to the stationers and bought things worth Rs. 25, out of which 30 paise went on sales tax on taxable purchases. If the tax rate was 6%, then what was the cost of the tax free items?**

- **A.** Rs. 15
- **B.** Rs. 15.70
- **C.** Rs. 19.70  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Rs. 20

**Explanation:** Let the amount taxable purchases be Rs. x . Then, 6% of x = 30 100 x = 30 x 100 = 5. 100 6 Cost of tax free items = Rs. [25 - (5 + 0.30)] = Rs. 19.70

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003)*

---

**Q32. A fires 5 shots to B's 3 but A kills only once in 3 shots while B kills once in 2 shots. When B has missed 27 times, A has killed:**

- **A.** 30 birds  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 60 birds
- **C.** 72 birds
- **D.** 90 birds

**Explanation:** Let the total number of shots be x . Then, Shots fired by A = 5 x 8 Shots fired by B = 3 x 8 Killing shots by A = 1 of 5 x = 5 x 3 8 24 Shots missed by B = 1 of 3 x = 3 x 2 8 16 3 x = 27 or x = 27 x 16 = 144. 16 3 Birds killed by A = 5 x = 5 x 144 = 30. 24 24

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002)*

---

**Q33. A fruit seller had some apples. He sells 40% apples and still has 420 apples. Originally, he had:**

- **A.** 588 apples
- **B.** 600 apples
- **C.** 672 apples
- **D.** 700 apples  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Suppose originally he had x apples. Then, (100 - 40)% of x = 420. 60 x x = 420 100 x = 420 x 100 = 700. 60 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/-Pv25Do3WwY

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/)*

---

**Q34. A grocer has a sale of Rs. 6435, Rs. 6927, Rs. 6855, Rs. 7230 and Rs. 6562 for 5 consecutive months. How much sale must he have in the sixth month so that he gets an average sale of Rs. 6500?**

- **A.** Rs. 4991  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Rs. 5991
- **C.** Rs. 6001
- **D.** Rs. 6991

**Explanation:** Total sale for 5 months = Rs. (6435 + 6927 + 6855 + 7230 + 6562) = Rs. 34009. Required sale = Rs. [ (6500 x 6) - 34009 ] = Rs. (39000 - 34009) = Rs. 4991. Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/tP4hszReksU

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/)*

---

**Q35. A man has some hens and cows. If the number of heads be 48 and the number of feet equals 140, then the number of hens will be:**

- **A.** 22
- **B.** 23
- **C.** 24
- **D.** 26  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let the number of hens be x and the number of cows be y . Then, x + y = 48 .... (i) and 2 x + 4 y = 140 x + 2 y = 70 .... (ii) Solving (i) and (ii) we get: x = 26, y = 22. The required answer = 26.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003)*

---

**Q36. The average age of husband, wife and their child 3 years ago was 27 years and that of wife and the child 5 years ago was 20 years. The present age of the husband is:**

- **A.** 35 years
- **B.** 40 years  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 50 years
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** Sum of the present ages of husband, wife and child = (27 x 3 + 3 x 3) years = 90 years. Sum of the present ages of wife and child = (20 x 2 + 5 x 2) years = 50 years. Husband's present age = (90 - 50) years = 40 years.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002)*

---

**Q37. What is the average age of children in the class? I. The age of the teacher is as many years as the number of children. II. Average age is increased by 1 year if the teacher's age is also included.**

- **A.** I alone sufficient while II alone not sufficient to answer
- **B.** II alone sufficient while I alone not sufficient to answer
- **C.** Either I or II alone sufficient to answer
- **D.** Both I and II are not sufficient to answer  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let there be x children. I gives, age of teacher = x years. II gives, average age of ( x + 1) persons = ( x + 1) years. Teacher's age = ( x + 1) ( x + 1) - x 2 = ( x 2 + 1 + 2 x ) - x 2 = (1 + 2 x ) Thus, teacher's age cannot be obtained. Correct answer is (D)

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/007001)*

---

**Q38. The price of 10 chairs is equal to that of 4 tables. The price of 15 chairs and 2 tables together is Rs. 4000. The total price of 12 chairs and 3 tables is:**

- **A.** Rs. 3500
- **B.** Rs. 3750
- **C.** Rs. 3840
- **D.** Rs. 3900  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let the cost of a chair and that of a table be Rs. x and Rs. y respectively. Then, 10 x = 4 y or y = 5 x . 2 15 x + 2 y = 4000 15 x + 2 x 5 x = 4000 2 20 x = 4000 x = 200. So, y = 5 x 200 = 500. 2 Hence, the cost of 12 chairs and 3 tables = 12 x + 3 y = Rs. (2400 + 1500) = Rs. 3900.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/)*

---

**Q39. A library has an average of 510 visitors on Sundays and 240 on other days. The average number of visitors per day in a month of 30 days beginning with a Sunday is:**

- **A.** 250
- **B.** 276
- **C.** 280
- **D.** 285  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Since the month begins with a Sunday, to there will be five Sundays in the month. Required average = 510 x 5 + 240 x 25 30 = 8550 30 = 285

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003)*

---

**Q40. Two numbers A and B are such that the sum of 5% of A and 4% of B is two-third of the sum of 6% of A and 8% of B. Find the ratio of A : B.**

- **A.** 2 : 3
- **B.** 1 : 1
- **C.** 3 : 4
- **D.** 4 : 3  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** 5% of A + 4% of B = 2 (6% of A + 8% of B) 3 5 A + 4 B = 2 6 A + 8 B 100 100 3 100 100 1 A + 1 B = 1 A + 4 B 20 25 25 75 1 - 1 A = 4 - 1 B 20 25 75 25 1 A = 1 B 100 75 A = 100 = 4 . B 75 3 Required ratio = 4 : 3 Video Explanation: YouTube Video

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002)*

---

## Aptitude — Standard 9

**Q41. A car owner buys petrol at Rs.7.50, Rs. 8 and Rs. 8.50 per litre for three successive years. What approximately is the average cost per litre of petrol if he spends Rs. 4000 each year?**

- **A.** Rs. 7.98  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Rs. 8
- **C.** Rs. 8.50
- **D.** Rs. 9

**Explanation:** Total quantity of petrol consumed in 3 years = 4000 + 4000 + 4000 litres 7.50 8 8.50 = 4000 2 + 1 + 2 litres 15 8 17 = 76700 litres 51 Total amount spent = Rs. (3 x 4000) = Rs. 12000. Average cost = Rs. 12000 x 51 = Rs. 6120 = Rs. 7.98 76700 767

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006002)*

---

**Q42. If a - b = 3 and a 2 + b 2 = 29, find the value of ab.**

- **A.** 10  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 12
- **C.** 15
- **D.** 18

**Explanation:** 2 ab = ( a 2 + b 2 ) - ( a - b ) 2 = 29 - 9 = 20 ab = 10.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/)*

---

**Q43. Eight people are planning to share equally the cost of a rental car. If one person withdraws from the arrangement and the others share equally the entire cost of the car, then the share of each of the remaining persons increased by:**

- **A.** 1 7  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 1 8
- **C.** 1 9
- **D.** 7 8

**Explanation:** Original share of 1 person = 1 8 New share of 1 person = 1 7 Increase = 1 - 1 = 1 7 8 56 Required fraction = (1/56) = 1 x 8 = 1 (1/8) 56 1 7

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004002)*

---

**Q44. If the average marks of three batches of 55, 60 and 45 students respectively is 50, 55, 60, then the average marks of all the students is:**

- **A.** 53.33
- **B.** 54.68  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 55
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** Required average = 55 x 50 + 60 x 55 + 45 x 60 55 + 60 + 45 = 2750 + 3300 + 2700 160 = 8750 160 = 54.68

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/006003)*

---

**Q45. A student multiplied a number by 3 instead of 5 . 5 3 What is the percentage error in the calculation?**

- **A.** 34%
- **B.** 44%
- **C.** 54%
- **D.** 64%  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Let the number be x . Then, error = 5 x - 3 x = 16 x . 3 5 15 Error% = 16 x x 3 x 100 % = 64%. 15 5 x

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017002)*

---

**Q46. What percentage of numbers from 1 to 70 have 1 or 9 in the unit's digit?**

- **A.** 1
- **B.** 14
- **C.** 20  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 21

**Explanation:** Clearly, the numbers which have 1 or 9 in the unit's digit, have squares that end in the digit 1. Such numbers from 1 to 70 are 1, 9, 11, 19, 21, 29, 31, 39, 41, 49, 51, 59, 61, 69. Number of such number =14 Required percentage = 14 x 100 % = 20%. 70 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/cBamI6iRNIA

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/)*

---

**Q47. How many marks did Tarun secure in English? I. The average mark obtained by Tarun in four subjects including English is 60. II. The total marks obtained by him in English and Mathematics together are 170. III. The total marks obtained by him in Mathematics and Science together are 180.**

- **A.** I and II only  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** II and III only
- **C.** I and III only
- **D.** All I, II and III

**Explanation:** I gives, total marks in 4 subjects = (60 x 4) = 240. II gives, E + M = 170 III gives, M + S = 180. Thus, none of (A), (B), (C), (D) is true. Correct answer is (E).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/008001](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/008001)*

---

**Q48. The average of 20 numbers is zero. Of them, at the most, how many may be greater than zero?**

- **A.** 0
- **B.** 1
- **C.** 10
- **D.** 19  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Average of 20 numbers = 0. Sum of 20 numbers (0 x 20) = 0. It is quite possible that 19 of these numbers may be positive and if their sum is a then 20th number is (- a ).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/average/)*

---

**Q49. Rajeev buys good worth Rs. 6650. He gets a rebate of 6% on it. After getting the rebate, he pays sales tax @ 10%. Find the amount he will have to pay for the goods.**

- **A.** Rs. 6876.10  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Rs. 6999.20
- **C.** Rs. 6654
- **D.** Rs. 7000

**Explanation:** Rebate = 6% of Rs. 6650 = Rs. 6 x 6650 = Rs. 399. 100 Sales tax = 10% of Rs. (6650 - 399) = Rs. 10 x 6251 = Rs. 625.10 100 Final amount = Rs. (6251 + 625.10) = Rs. 6876.10 Video Explanation: https://youtu.be/XuiggCTh3SU

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/percentage/017003)*

---

**Q50. (469 + 174) 2 - (469 - 174) 2 = ? (469 x 174)**

- **A.** 2
- **B.** 4  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 295
- **D.** 643

**Explanation:** Given exp. = ( a + b ) 2 - ( a - b ) 2 ab = 4 ab ab = 4 (where a = 469, b = 174.)

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003](https://www.indiabix.com/aptitude/simplification/004003)*

---

## Biology — Standard 10

**Q51. What is the wavelength of visible spectrum?**

- **A.** 8500 - 9800 angstrom
- **B.** 7800 - 8000 angstrom
- **C.** 3900 - 7600 angstrom  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 1300 - 3000 angstrom

**Explanation:** The visible spectrum refers to the range of electromagnetic radiation that human eyes can detect, spanning from violet light (shortest wavelength) to red light (longest wavelength), which corresponds to approximately 3900–7600 angstroms. Why not the others: (A) 8500–9800 angstrom falls in the infrared region, beyond visible red light; (B) 7800–8000 angstrom also extends into infrared and misses much of the visible range; (D) 1300–3000 angstrom falls in the ultraviolet region, which is not visible to human eyes.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002)*

---

**Q52. Which of the following metals forms an amalgam with other metals?**

- **A.** Tin
- **B.** Mercury  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Lead
- **D.** Zinc

**Explanation:** Mercury is a liquid metal at room temperature that readily dissolves and forms alloys called amalgams with other metals, making it unique among common metals. Why not the others: (A) Tin forms alloys like bronze but not true amalgams; (C) Lead forms alloys but does not characteristically form amalgams; (D) Zinc is a solid metal that forms brass and other alloys, not amalgams.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/)*

---

**Q53. Bromine is a**

- **A.** black solid
- **B.** red liquid  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** colourless gas
- **D.** highly inflammable gas

**Explanation:** Bromine is a red liquid at room temperature, with a distinctive reddish-brown colour and volatile nature that causes it to evaporate easily, producing a pungent vapour. Why not the others: (A) black solid — bromine is not a solid at standard conditions; (C) colourless gas — bromine is not colourless and exists as a liquid, not gas, at room temperature; (D) highly inflammable gas — bromine is neither a gas nor highly inflammable in the traditional sense, though it is reactive.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036003)*

---

**Q54. Pigmentation of skin is due to**

- **A.** lymphocytes
- **B.** monocytes
- **C.** leucocytes
- **D.** melanocytes  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Melanocytes are specialized cells located in the basal layer of the epidermis that produce melanin, the primary pigment responsible for skin color and protection against UV radiation. Why not the others: (A) lymphocytes are white blood cells involved in immune responses, not pigmentation; (B) monocytes are immune cells that differentiate into macrophages, not related to skin color; (C) leucocytes are general white blood cells with immune functions, not pigment-producing cells.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018011](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018011)*

---

**Q55. Placenta is the structure formed**

- **A.** by the union of foetal and uterine tissue  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** by foetus only
- **C.** by fusion of germ layers
- **D.** None of these

**Explanation:** The placenta is a composite organ formed by the union of fetal tissue (specifically the chorion and allantois) and maternal uterine tissue (the endometrium), making it a structure that develops from contributions of both mother and fetus. This dual origin allows the placenta to facilitate nutrient and gas exchange between maternal and fetal circulation. Why not the others: (B) the fetus alone cannot form the placenta without maternal tissue contribution; (C) while the placenta involves germ layers, it is fundamentally defined by its mixed fetal-maternal origin rather than germ layer fusion alone; (D) option A is correct, so 'None of these' is inappropriate.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018006](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018006)*

---

**Q56. Primary phloem develops from**

- **A.** lateral meristem
- **B.** protoderm
- **C.** extrastelar cambium
- **D.** provascular tissue  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Primary phloem develops from provascular tissue (procambium), which is a primary meristem that differentiates into both primary xylem and primary phloem during the initial growth of plants. Why not the others: (A) lateral meristem produces secondary growth tissues like secondary phloem, not primary phloem; (B) protoderm forms the epidermis, not vascular tissues; (C) extrastelar cambium is not a recognized meristematic tissue involved in phloem formation.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018005](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018005)*

---

**Q57. Very small time intervals are accurately measure by**

- **A.** White dwarfs
- **B.** Quartz clocks
- **C.** Atomic clocks  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Pulsars

**Explanation:** Atomic clocks measure time using the vibrations of atoms (typically cesium-133) at extremely precise and constant frequencies, making them the most accurate instruments for measuring very small time intervals, with accuracy to nanoseconds or better. Why not the others: (A) white dwarfs are astronomical objects, not timekeeping devices; (B) quartz clocks are far less precise than atomic clocks, accurate only to milliseconds; (D) pulsars are neutron stars used as cosmic clocks but are less precise than laboratory atomic clocks for measuring small intervals.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002)*

---

**Q58. Fathom is the unit of**

- **A.** sound
- **B.** depth  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** frequency
- **D.** distance

**Explanation:** A fathom is a unit of measurement primarily used to measure the depth of water, particularly in maritime and nautical contexts, where it equals approximately 6 feet or 1.83 meters. Why not the others: (A) sound is measured in decibels or hertz, not fathoms; (C) frequency is measured in hertz or cycles per second, not fathoms; (D) while depth is a form of distance, fathom is specifically designated for measuring water depth in nautical applications, not general distance.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038001)*

---

**Q59. Plants are killed in winter by frost**

- **A.** because of desiccation and mechanical damage to the tissues  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** because no photosynthesis takes place at such low temperature
- **C.** because respiration ceases at such low temperature
- **D.** because there is no transpiration

**Explanation:** Frost kills plants primarily through desiccation (removal of water from cells) and mechanical damage when ice crystals form within plant tissues, causing cell walls and membranes to rupture. Why not the others: (B) while photosynthesis does slow at low temperatures, many plants survive winter dormancy without photosynthesis occurring; (C) respiration continues at low temperatures, albeit at reduced rates, and is not the primary killing mechanism; (D) lack of transpiration alone does not kill plants—many dormant plants survive winter with minimal transpiration.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018014](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018014)*

---

**Q60. Most common disease of poultry in India is**

- **A.** fowl pox
- **B.** tick fever
- **C.** ranikhet  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** coryza

**Explanation:** Ranikhet disease, also known as Newcastle disease, is the most common and economically significant viral disease affecting poultry in India, causing high mortality rates and substantial losses to the poultry industry. Why not the others: (A) fowl pox is a viral disease but less prevalent and economically damaging than ranikhet; (B) tick fever primarily affects cattle and not poultry; (D) coryza (infectious coryza) is a bacterial disease in poultry but far less common and severe compared to ranikhet.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018013](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018013)*

---

**Q61. Plants receive their nutrients mainly from**

- **A.** chlorophyll
- **B.** atmosphere
- **C.** light
- **D.** soil  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Plants absorb essential macronutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium primarily through their root systems from the soil, along with micronutrients and water necessary for growth and metabolism. Why not the others: (A) chlorophyll is a pigment that captures light for photosynthesis, not a nutrient source; (B) while plants do obtain carbon and oxygen from the atmosphere, most of their nutrient requirements come from soil; (C) light is an energy source for photosynthesis, not a nutrient in the chemical sense.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/)*

---

**Q62. Out of 900 reported species of living gymnosperms, conifers are represented by about 500 species, About 2,50,000 species of angiosperms (flowering plants) have also been reported in the world. The vast and dominant woodlands in Europe, Asia, North America and mountains such as Himalayas are wooded with**

- **A.** all gymnosperms, except conifers
- **B.** only angiosperms
- **C.** only conifers  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** angiosperms and all gymnosperms except conifers

**Explanation:** The vast and dominant woodlands in Europe, Asia, North America, and the Himalayas are primarily composed of conifers because conifers are exceptionally well-adapted to cool and temperate climates, featuring needle-like leaves, thick bark, and growth forms that withstand harsh environmental conditions in these regions. Conifers dominate these geographical zones and form the characteristic boreal and temperate forests found across the Northern Hemisphere and high-altitude mountains. Why not the others: (A) non-coniferous gymnosperms like cycads and ginkgos are relatively rare and do not form the dominant woodlands in these regions; (B) angiosperms, while diverse and abundant, do not dominate the cooler, high-altitude, or far-northern forests that characterize these specific landscapes; (D) combining angiosperms with non-coniferous gymnosperms misrepresents the actual composition, as these groups are not the primary constituents of the woodlands described.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018003)*

---

**Q63. Wood is the main raw material for the manufacture of**

- **A.** paint
- **B.** paper  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** ink
- **D.** gun powder

**Explanation:** Wood is the primary raw material used in paper manufacturing because cellulose fibers from wood pulp form the structural basis of paper products. Why not the others: (A) paint is made primarily from resins, pigments, and solvents rather than wood; (C) ink is produced from dyes, oils, and other chemical compounds, not wood as a main raw material; (D) gunpowder is a chemical mixture of saltpeter, charcoal, and sulfur, where wood is only a minor component used to make charcoal.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002)*

---

**Q64. Isotopes are separated by**

- **A.** crystallisation
- **B.** sublimation
- **C.** distillation  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** filtration

**Explanation:** Distillation is the correct method because isotopes have different mass numbers, leading to slight differences in their physical properties like boiling points, which allows them to be separated when a mixture is heated and the vapors are condensed at different temperatures. Why not the others: (A) crystallization separates compounds based on solubility differences, not isotopic mass variations; (B) sublimation is used for substances that convert directly from solid to gas, not for isotope separation; (D) filtration separates solids from liquids or particles by size, which cannot distinguish between isotopes of the same element.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001)*

---

**Q65. One day you wake with a sore throat and a runny nose. Your doctor takes a swab from your throat, sends it to a lab, and telephones you the next day to say that antibiotic will not help you get better. Which of the following is the most likely reason for the doctor's statement?**

- **A.** Having waited a day, it is too late to take an antibiotic
- **B.** You need an antiseptic, not an antibiotic
- **C.** You need to be vaccinated instead of taking an antibiotic
- **D.** You are infected by a virus  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** A sore throat and runny nose with a lab result indicating antibiotics won't help indicates a viral infection, because antibiotics are ineffective against viruses—they only work against bacteria. The doctor's determination came from analyzing the throat swab, which would have revealed whether the infection was bacterial or viral. Why not the others: (A) antibiotics can be effective at any stage of infection if the pathogen is bacterial; (B) antiseptics kill microbes on surfaces but are not taken internally for systemic infections; (C) vaccination prevents future infections but does not treat current symptoms of active infection.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015)*

---

**Q66. Knot is a unit of speed of which of the following?**

- **A.** Aeroplane
- **B.** Light waves
- **C.** Ship  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Sound waves

**Explanation:** A knot is a unit of speed used to measure the velocity of ships and vessels, equal to one nautical mile per hour. This unit originated from maritime tradition and remains the standard for measuring naval and marine vessel speeds. Why not the others: (A) Aeroplanes use kilometers per hour or miles per hour, not knots as their standard speed measurement; (B) Light waves are measured in terms of speed in meters per second or as a fraction of the speed of light, not knots; (D) Sound waves are measured in meters per second or feet per second, not knots.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038004)*

---

**Q67. Organic Substances which, in very small amounts, control growth and development called**

- **A.** vitamins
- **B.** hormones  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** enzymes
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** Hormones are organic substances produced in small amounts by endocrine glands that regulate growth, development, and various physiological processes throughout the body. They act as chemical messengers that control metabolism, reproduction, and homeostasis in living organisms. Why not the others: (A) vitamins are micronutrients needed for metabolic functions but don't specifically control growth and development as their primary role; (C) enzymes are proteins that catalyze biochemical reactions rather than regulate growth and development; (D) there is a correct biological term for this definition.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018012](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018012)*

---

**Q68. Plants absorb dissolved nitrates from soil and convert them into**

- **A.** free nitrogen  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** urea
- **C.** ammonia
- **D.** proteins

**Explanation:** Plants absorb dissolved nitrates from soil and convert them into free nitrogen through a process called denitrification, which occurs in their root systems and tissues. Why not the others: (B) urea is a nitrogenous waste product produced by animals, not the primary form that plants convert nitrates into; (C) ammonia is an intermediate compound in some metabolic pathways but not the final product of nitrate absorption in plants; (D) proteins are complex organic molecules synthesized by plants using nitrogen, but they are not the direct conversion product of dissolved nitrates—nitrogen must first be converted to a simpler form before protein synthesis occurs.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018002)*

---

**Q69. Detergents used for cleaning clothes and utensils contain?**

- **A.** bicarbonates
- **B.** bismuthates
- **C.** sulphonates  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** nitrates

**Explanation:** Detergents contain sulphonates (or sulphonic acid salts) as their primary active ingredient because these molecules have both hydrophobic and hydrophilic portions that allow them to break down grease and oils, making them effective cleaning agents. Why not the others: (A) bicarbonates are weak bases used in baking and antacids, not primary detergent components; (B) bismuthates are oxidizing salts with no role in cleaning formulations; (D) nitrates are oxidizing salts used as fertilizers and explosives, not detergent ingredients.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040001)*

---

**Q70. Which of the gas is not known as green house gas?**

- **A.** Methane
- **B.** Nitrous oxide
- **C.** Carbon dioxide
- **D.** Hydrogen  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** In order, the most abundant greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are: * water vapor * carbon dioxide * methane * nitrous oxide * ozone * chlorofluorocarbons

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002)*

---

**Q71. out of proteins, lipids and carbohydrates present in a cell membrane, what is true?**

- **A.** Lipids are maximum
- **B.** Carbohydrates are minimum  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Carbohydrates are maximum
- **D.** All three are in equal proportion

**Explanation:** The cell membrane is primarily composed of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins, while carbohydrates are present only as parts of glycoproteins and glycolipids on the membrane surface. By mass, proteins typically comprise about 50% and lipids about 50% of the membrane, but carbohydrates make up only 2-10%, making them the minimum component. Why not the others: (A) while lipids form the basic bilayer structure, proteins often equal or slightly exceed lipid content by mass; (C) carbohydrates are actually a minor structural component, not the maximum; (D) the three macromolecules are definitely not present in equal proportions in the membrane.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018007](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018007)*

---

**Q72. Most highly intelligent mammals are**

- **A.** whales
- **B.** dolphins  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** elephants
- **D.** kangaroos

**Explanation:** Dolphins are among the most highly intelligent mammals, possessing large brains relative to body size, complex social structures, self-awareness, problem-solving abilities, and sophisticated communication systems that rival those of great apes. Why not the others: (A) whales, while intelligent, generally have smaller brain-to-body ratios and less evidence of complex cognitive abilities compared to dolphins; (C) elephants, though highly intelligent with strong social bonds and memory, do not demonstrate the same level of cognitive complexity and adaptability as dolphins; (D) kangaroos lack the neurological complexity and behavioral evidence of high intelligence shown by dolphins and other species.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018004)*

---

## Biology — Standard 6

**Q73. Plants hormone that induces cell division is**

- **A.** auxins
- **B.** gibberellins
- **C.** kinins  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** domins

**Explanation:** Kinins (or cytokinins) are plant hormones that promote cell division and growth, particularly in shoots and leaves, making them the primary hormone responsible for inducing mitotic cell division. Why not the others: (A) auxins promote cell elongation and growth rather than cell division; (B) gibberellins stimulate seed germination and stem elongation, not specifically cell division; (D) domins is not a recognized plant hormone.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018006](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018006)*

---

**Q74. What is the unit for measuring the amplitude of a sound?**

- **A.** Decibel  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Coulomb
- **C.** Hum
- **D.** Cycles

**Explanation:** Amplitude of sound, which represents the intensity or loudness of a sound wave, is measured in decibels (dB), a logarithmic unit that quantifies sound pressure level relative to a reference value. Why not the others: (B) Coulomb is the unit of electric charge, not sound amplitude; (C) Hum is not a standardized unit of measurement in physics; (D) Cycles refers to frequency (cycles per second or Hertz), not amplitude.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038001)*

---

**Q75. Our major foods, fibres, spices, fruits and beverage crops are**

- **A.** flowering plants  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** gymnosperms plants
- **C.** pteridophytes
- **D.** bryophytes

**Explanation:** Flowering plants (angiosperms) produce seeds enclosed in fruits and have highly specialized structures for reproduction, making them the primary source of human food crops (wheat, rice, fruits), fibres (cotton), spices (pepper, turmeric), and beverages (tea, coffee). Why not the others: (B) gymnosperms like conifers produce naked seeds and are not sources of major food or beverage crops; (C) pteridophytes are seedless vascular plants used neither for food nor as major commercial crops; (D) bryophytes are non-vascular plants without seeds or roots and have no role in providing human food, fibres, or spices.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018012](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018012)*

---

**Q76. Which of the following rays are more penetrating?**

- **A.** Beta rays
- **B.** Alpha rays
- **C.** Gamma rays  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** X-rays

**Explanation:** Gamma rays are the most penetrating form of radiation because they are high-energy electromagnetic waves with no charge or mass, allowing them to pass through matter with minimal interaction. Why not the others: (A) Beta rays have less penetrating power than gamma rays as they are charged particles that can be stopped by a few millimeters of aluminum; (B) Alpha rays have the lowest penetrating power and can be stopped by a sheet of paper or human skin; (D) X-rays are penetrating but less so than gamma rays as they have lower energy levels and can be blocked by dense materials like lead more easily.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039005](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039005)*

---

**Q77. Brass gets discoloured in air because of the presence of which of the following gases in air?**

- **A.** Oxygen
- **B.** Hydrogen sulphide  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Carbon dioxide
- **D.** Nitrogen

**Explanation:** Brass, an alloy of copper and zinc, gets discoloured in air primarily due to hydrogen sulphide (H₂S) gas, which reacts with the copper component to form a dark layer of copper sulphide on the surface. Why not the others: (A) Oxygen alone causes only slow oxidation and does not produce the characteristic dark discolouration associated with brass tarnishing; (C) Carbon dioxide does not chemically react with brass to cause discolouration; (D) Nitrogen is inert and does not react with brass under normal atmospheric conditions.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/)*

---

**Q78. Ordinary table salt is sodium chloride. What is baking soda?**

- **A.** Potassium chloride
- **B.** Potassium carbonate
- **C.** Potassium hydroxide
- **D.** Sodium bicarbonate  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Baking soda is sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO₃), a weak base commonly used in cooking and baking as a leavening agent that produces carbon dioxide gas when heated or mixed with acids. Why not the others: (A) Potassium chloride is a salt similar to table salt, not a leavening agent; (B) Potassium carbonate is a strong alkali used industrially, not for baking; (C) Potassium hydroxide is a caustic chemical unsuitable for food use.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/)*

---

**Q79. Movement of cell against concentration gradient is called**

- **A.** osmosis
- **B.** active transport  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** diffusion
- **D.** passive transport

**Explanation:** Active transport, this is the only transport method that can move species against their concentration gradient (from low to high concentration). Facilitated diffusion only moves species down their concentration gradient from high to low concentration.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018002)*

---

**Q80. The hardest substance available on earth is**

- **A.** Gold
- **B.** Iron
- **C.** Diamond  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Platinum

**Explanation:** Diamond is the hardest naturally occurring substance on Earth because of its crystal lattice structure, where each carbon atom is bonded to four other carbon atoms in an extremely strong tetrahedral arrangement, making it incredibly resistant to scratching and wear. Why not the others: (A) Gold is a soft, malleable metal used in jewelry; (B) Iron is relatively hard but far less hard than diamond and can rust; (D) Platinum is a dense precious metal but is softer than diamond.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036003)*

---

**Q81. Pollen grains in plants are produced in**

- **A.** roots
- **B.** leaves
- **C.** flower  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** stem

**Explanation:** Pollen grains are produced in the anthers of flowers, which are the male reproductive structures of flowering plants. The anther is the pollen-bearing part of the stamen located within the flower. Why not the others: (A) roots are specialized for water and nutrient absorption, not pollen production; (B) leaves are the site of photosynthesis and gas exchange, not pollen formation; (D) stems provide structural support and transport, but pollen is not produced there.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018016](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018016)*

---

**Q82. Plant development is influenced by**

- **A.** quality of light only
- **B.** quality and quantity of light
- **C.** quality and duration of light
- **D.** quality, quantity and duration of light  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Plant development is influenced by all three factors of light: quality (wavelength/color), quantity (intensity), and duration (photoperiod). Quality affects photosynthesis and photomorphogenesis, quantity determines the rate of light-dependent processes, and duration controls flowering, germination, and other photoperiodic responses. Why not the others: (A) ignores the critical roles of quantity and duration in plant physiology; (B) overlooks the essential role of light duration (photoperiod) in controlling flowering and developmental timing; (C) neglects light intensity/quantity, which is necessary for adequate photosynthesis and growth.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018004)*

---

**Q83. Other than spreading malaria, anopheles mosquitoes are also vectors of**

- **A.** dengue fever
- **B.** filariasis  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** encephalitis
- **D.** yellow fever

**Explanation:** Anopheles mosquitoes are the primary vectors of filariasis (lymphatic filariasis), a parasitic disease caused by nematode worms transmitted through their bites. Besides malaria, filariasis is one of the major diseases spread by this genus of mosquitoes. Why not the others: (A) dengue fever is transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes, not Anopheles; (C) encephalitis can be spread by various vectors including Culex mosquitoes, but not characteristically by Anopheles; (D) yellow fever is transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes, particularly Aedes aegypti, not Anopheles.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018005](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018005)*

---

**Q84. Which of the following metals remain in liquid for under normal conditions?**

- **A.** Radium
- **B.** Zinc
- **C.** Uranium
- **D.** Mercury  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Mercury is the only metal that exists as a liquid at room temperature (normal conditions), with a melting point of approximately −39°C, making it liquid under standard atmospheric conditions. Why not the others: (A) Radium is a solid metal with a very high melting point; (B) Zinc is a solid metal at room temperature with a melting point around 420°C; (C) Uranium is a solid metal at room temperature with a melting point around 1,132°C.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036013](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036013)*

---

**Q85. Most abundant tissues of our body are**

- **A.** muscular
- **B.** connective  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** epithelial
- **D.** nervous

**Explanation:** Connective tissue is the most abundant tissue in the human body by mass and volume, comprising approximately 40% of body weight and including bone, cartilage, adipose tissue, and blood, which collectively occupy more space than any other tissue type. Why not the others: (A) muscular tissue, though important, makes up only about 40% of body mass and is less abundant overall than connective tissue; (C) epithelial tissue covers surfaces but forms thin layers and comprises a much smaller percentage of total body volume; (D) nervous tissue is highly specialized but represents a small fraction of total body mass and volume.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018013](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018013)*

---

**Q86. Rayon is chemically**

- **A.** cellulose  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** pectin
- **C.** glucose
- **D.** amylase

**Explanation:** Rayon is chemically cellulose because it is a semi-synthetic fiber made by chemically processing natural cellulose (obtained from wood pulp or cotton linters) through the viscose or other processes, converting it into a fiber form while maintaining its cellulose composition. Why not the others: (B) pectin is a polysaccharide found in plant cell walls but is not the chemical basis of rayon; (C) glucose is a simple sugar monomer, not the polymer from which rayon is derived; (D) amylase is an enzyme that breaks down starch, not a chemical component of rayon.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002)*

---

**Q87. One kilometre is equal to how many miles?**

- **A.** 0.84
- **B.** 0.5
- **C.** 1.6
- **D.** 0.62  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** One kilometre is equal to approximately 0.62 miles, derived from the conversion factor where 1 km = 0.621371 miles. This is the standard metric-to-imperial conversion used globally. Why not the others: (A) 0.84 is closer to the reciprocal relationship but inverted incorrectly; (B) 0.5 significantly underestimates the mile equivalent of a kilometre; (C) 1.6 represents the inverse conversion—how many kilometres equal one mile, not the reverse.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002)*

---

**Q88. Which is/are the important raw material(s) required in cement industry?**

- **A.** Gypsum and Clay
- **B.** Clay
- **C.** Limestone and Clay  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Limestone

**Explanation:** Limestone and clay are the two primary raw materials used in cement manufacturing, as limestone provides calcium carbonate while clay supplies silica and alumina, which are essential for producing Portland cement. Why not the others: (A) gypsum is added later as a retarder during cement grinding, not as a primary raw material; (B) clay alone is insufficient and requires limestone as the major component; (D) limestone alone cannot produce cement without clay to supply silica and alumina.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040001)*

---

**Q89. One of the following is not a function of bones.**

- **A.** Place for muscle attachment
- **B.** Protection of vital organs
- **C.** Secretion of hormones for calcium regulation in blood and bones  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Production of blood corpuscles

**Explanation:** Option C is correct because bones do not secrete hormones; rather, the parathyroid glands secrete parathyroid hormone (PTH) to regulate calcium levels in blood and bones. Bones have several key functions: they provide attachment sites for muscles (A), protect vital organs like the heart, lungs, and brain (B), and contain bone marrow that produces red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets (D). Why not the others: (A) muscles attach to bones for movement and leverage; (B) the rib cage and skull are crucial protective structures; (D) bone marrow is the primary site of hematopoiesis (blood cell production).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018003)*

---

**Q90. The wavelength of X-rays is of the order of**

- **A.** 10 micron
- **B.** 1 angstrom  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 1 cm
- **D.** 1 m

**Explanation:** 1 angstrom = 1.0 x 10 -10 meters

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002)*

---

**Q91. One of the following is most suitable for study of mutations**

- **A.** Haploids  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Diploids
- **C.** Tetraploids
- **D.** Polyploid

**Explanation:** Haploids are most suitable for studying mutations because they contain only one copy of each chromosome, so any mutation in a gene is immediately expressed and easily observable phenotypically, without being masked by dominant alleles on a homologous chromosome. This makes it simple to detect and analyze the effects of mutations directly. Why not the others: (B) Diploids have two chromosome copies, so recessive mutations may be hidden by dominant alleles, making them harder to detect; (C) Tetraploids have four chromosome sets, creating even greater genetic redundancy and masking effects; (D) Polyploids is too broad a term and suffers from the same masking problem as other organisms with multiple chromosome copies.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018014](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018014)*

---

**Q92. Norepinephrine increases**

- **A.** respiration
- **B.** urine production
- **C.** saliva production
- **D.** blood pressure  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Norepinephrine is a catecholamine that acts as both a neurotransmitter and hormone, primarily causing vasoconstriction and increasing heart rate, which directly raises blood pressure. Why not the others: (A) while norepinephrine may have some indirect effects on respiration, it does not primarily increase respiratory rate; (B) norepinephrine actually decreases urine production by constricting renal blood vessels; (C) norepinephrine reduces saliva production as part of the sympathetic fight-or-flight response.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018011](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018011)*

---

**Q93. Chemical formula for water is**

- **A.** NaAlO 2
- **B.** H 2 O  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Al 2 O 3
- **D.** CaSiO 3

**Explanation:** Water is a simple compound formed from two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom, represented by the chemical formula H₂O. This is the fundamental molecular composition of water found in nature. Why not the others: (A) NaAlO₂ is sodium aluminate, an inorganic compound used in water treatment; (C) Al₂O₃ is aluminum oxide, a ceramic and abrasive material; (D) CaSiO₃ is calcium silicate, a compound found in cement and minerals.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002)*

---

**Q94. Plants that grow under average temperature and moisture are called**

- **A.** halophytes
- **B.** hydrophytes
- **C.** mesophytes  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** xerophytes

**Explanation:** Mesophytes are plants adapted to grow in environments with moderate or average temperature and moisture conditions, making them suited to typical terrestrial habitats. Why not the others: (A) halophytes are salt-tolerant plants that grow in saline environments; (B) hydrophytes are aquatic or water-loving plants adapted to wet conditions; (D) xerophytes are drought-resistant plants adapted to dry environments with low moisture.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015)*

---

**Q95. Potato is a modified form (outgrowth) of**

- **A.** root
- **B.** stem  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** fruit
- **D.** leaf

**Explanation:** A potato is a modified underground stem called a tuber, not a root or other organ. It develops from stolons (horizontal stems) and stores starch as a food reserve, which is characteristic of stem tissue. Why not the others: (A) roots lack nodes and buds, while potatoes have both; (C) fruits are reproductive structures that develop from flowers, potatoes are not; (D) leaves are photosynthetic organs and lack the internal structure of a potato.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018007](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018007)*

---

**Q96. Which of the following is used as a moderator in nuclear reactor?**

- **A.** Thorium
- **B.** Graphite  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Radium
- **D.** Ordinary water

**Explanation:** Graphite is used as a moderator in nuclear reactors because it effectively slows down fast neutrons to thermal speeds, enabling sustained nuclear fission while being neutron-efficient and heat-resistant. Why not the others: (A) Thorium is a fissile material itself, not a moderator; (C) Radium is highly radioactive and unstable, unsuitable for reactor use; (D) Ordinary water absorbs too many neutrons to be an effective moderator in most reactor designs, though heavy water is sometimes used.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001)*

---

## Biology — Standard 7

**Q97. Oxyreductases, transferases, hydrolases, lyases, isomerases and ligases are all classes of**

- **A.** hormones
- **B.** enzymes  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** proteins
- **D.** vitamins

**Explanation:** The six categories listed—oxyreductases, transferases, hydrolases, lyases, isomerases, and ligases—represent the six major classes of enzymes according to the EC (Enzyme Commission) classification system, each catalyzing different types of biochemical reactions. This is the standard enzyme taxonomy used in biochemistry. Why not the others: (A) hormones are signaling molecules, not a classification system for catalytic proteins; (C) while enzymes are proteins, not all proteins are classified into these six catalytic categories; (D) vitamins are organic micronutrients, not enzymatic catalysts classified this way.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018015)*

---

**Q98. The gas usually filled in the electric bulb is**

- **A.** nitrogen  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** hydrogen
- **C.** carbon dioxide
- **D.** oxygen

**Explanation:** Electric bulbs are filled with nitrogen gas because it is inert and does not react with the hot tungsten filament, preventing oxidation and extending the bulb's lifespan. Why not the others: (B) hydrogen is highly flammable and would react dangerously with the hot filament; (C) carbon dioxide would react with the heated filament and reduce its efficiency; (D) oxygen would rapidly oxidize the tungsten filament, causing it to burn out quickly.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/036002)*

---

**Q99. Nitrogen is fixed in ecosystems in ways stated below. Which one of the statements below is false?**

- **A.** By cyanobacteria
- **B.** By electrical discharges in the atmosphere
- **C.** By industrially synthesised fertilizer
- **D.** By denitrification  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Denitrification is the process by which nitrates in soil are converted back into nitrogen gas and released into the atmosphere, which is the opposite of nitrogen fixation. Nitrogen fixation refers to the conversion of atmospheric nitrogen (N₂) into usable forms like ammonia or nitrates, which is accomplished by cyanobacteria through biological processes, by lightning and electrical discharges through atmospheric chemistry, and by industrial processes in fertilizer production. Why not the others: (A) cyanobacteria are major biological nitrogen fixers in aquatic and terrestrial ecosystems; (B) electrical discharges in the atmosphere provide energy to convert N₂ into nitrogen oxides that eventually form nitrates; (C) industrial synthesis (the Haber-Bosch process) artificially fixes atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia for fertilizers.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018016](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/018016)*

---

**Q100. How many colours the sunlight spectrum has?**

- **A.** Three
- **B.** Seven  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Four
- **D.** Five

**Explanation:** Sunlight, when dispersed through a prism or refracted by water droplets, separates into seven distinct colours commonly remembered by the acronym VIBGYOR: Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, and Red. This seven-colour spectrum was famously identified by Isaac Newton through his prism experiments. Why not the others: (A) Three colours is too few and does not account for the full range of visible light wavelengths; (C) Four colours is an incomplete division of the spectrum; (D) Five colours also omits portions of the spectrum that are distinctly visible.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039005](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039005)*

---

**Q101. Mesons are found in**

- **A.** Laser beam
- **B.** X-rays
- **C.** Gamma rays
- **D.** Cosmic rays  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Mesons are subatomic particles that are produced when cosmic rays collide with atoms in Earth's atmosphere, making them detectable in cosmic ray showers. Cosmic rays provide the high-energy environment necessary for meson creation, which is why they are found in cosmic rays. Why not the others: (A) Laser beams are coherent light and contain only photons, not mesons; (B) X-rays are electromagnetic radiation (high-energy photons) without mesons; (C) Gamma rays are also electromagnetic radiation and high-energy photons, not composed of mesons.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039002)*

---

**Q102. kilohertz is a unit which measures**

- **A.** power used by a current of one ampere
- **B.** electromagnetic radio wave frequencies  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** voltage
- **D.** electric resistance

**Explanation:** A kilohertz (kHz) is a unit of frequency equal to 1,000 cycles per second, and is commonly used to measure the frequency of electromagnetic radio waves and other oscillating phenomena. Why not the others: (A) power is measured in watts, not kilohertz; (C) voltage is measured in volts, not kilohertz; (D) electric resistance is measured in ohms, not kilohertz.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/038002)*

---

**Q103. Ozone hole refers to**

- **A.** hole in ozone layer
- **B.** decrease in the ozone layer in troposphere
- **C.** decrease in thickness of ozone layer in stratosphere  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** increase in the thickness of ozone layer in troposphere

**Explanation:** The ozone hole refers to a significant decrease in the thickness of the ozone layer located in the stratosphere, primarily caused by chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) and other ozone-depleting substances. This thinning allows increased ultraviolet radiation to reach Earth's surface. Why not the others: (A) is too vague and doesn't specify the stratosphere or the nature of the depletion; (B) incorrectly identifies the troposphere as the location of the ozone layer, when the ozone layer is actually in the stratosphere; (D) describes an increase rather than a decrease, and places it in the wrong atmospheric layer.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/biology/)*

---

**Q104. Which among the following is a positively charged particle emitted by a radioactive element?**

- **A.** Beta ray
- **B.** Alpha ray  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Cathode ray
- **D.** Gamma ray

**Explanation:** An alpha ray (or alpha particle) is a positively charged particle consisting of two protons and two neutrons (helium nucleus) emitted during radioactive decay of heavy elements. Why not the others: (A) Beta rays are negatively charged electrons emitted from the nucleus; (C) Cathode rays are electrons produced in vacuum tubes, not from radioactive elements; (D) Gamma rays are electromagnetic radiation with no charge.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/039001)*

---

**Q105. Optic fibres are mainly used for which of the following?**

- **A.** Musical instruments
- **B.** Food industry
- **C.** Weaving
- **D.** Communication  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Optic fibres are thin strands of glass or plastic that transmit data as pulses of light, making them ideal for high-speed, long-distance communication networks including internet, telephone, and television transmission. Why not the others: (A) musical instruments do not rely on optic fibres as their primary technology; (B) the food industry uses optic fibres minimally and they are not a main application; (C) weaving is a textile process that does not depend on optic fibre technology.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/general-science/040002)*

---

**Q106. The variety of coal in which the deposit contains recognisable traces of the original plant material is**

- **A.** bitumen
- **B.** anthracite
- **C.** lignite
- **D.** peat  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Peat is the least altered form of coal, representing the earliest stage of coal formation, and it retains clearly visible traces of the original plant material from which it was formed. Why not the others: (A) bitumen is a liquid hydrocarbon product, not a coal variety; (B) anthracite is the most metamorphosed coal with no recognizable plant material due to extreme heat and pressure; (C) lignite is partially decomposed coal but has lost most visible traces of original plant structures compared to peat.
