*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001)*

**Q288. For which of the following disciplines is Nobel Prize awarded?**

- **A.** Physics and Chemistry
- **B.** Physiology or Medicine
- **C.** Literature, Peace and Economics
- **D.** All of the above  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The Nobel Prize is awarded across six disciplines: Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, Literature, Peace, and Economic Sciences. Since option (D) encompasses all of these categories by stating 'All of the above,' it is the most complete and correct answer. Why not the others: (A) lists only two scientific disciplines and omits Medicine, Literature, Peace, and Economics; (B) covers only one discipline, leaving out Physics, Chemistry, Literature, Peace, and Economics; (C) includes three categories but omits the scientific disciplines of Physics, Chemistry, and Medicine.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/)*

**Q289. The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Award is given by which of the following organisations?**

- **A.** Council for Scientific and industrial research  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Indian Council for Agricultural Research
- **C.** Indian National Science Academy
- **D.** ISRO

**Explanation:** The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize for Science and Technology is awarded annually by the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), which is India's premier R&D organization, to recognize outstanding research contributions in science and technology. Why not the others: (B) ICAR focuses on agricultural research and does not administer this award; (C) INSA is a prestigious science academy but does not confer this particular prize; (D) ISRO is a space agency and is not responsible for this national science award.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007)*

**Q290. How many players are there in Kabbadi team?**

- **A.** 11
- **B.** 9
- **C.** 5
- **D.** 7  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** A Kabbadi team has 7 players on the playing court at any given time during a match. This is the standard format used in both national and international Kabbadi competitions. Why not the others: (A) 11 is the number of players in a football/soccer team, not Kabbadi; (B) 9 is incorrect and does not correspond to any major team sport; (C) 5 is the number of players in basketball, not Kabbadi.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028001)*

**Q291. Which of the following societies has instituted an award for an outstanding parliamentarian?**

- **A.** Jamanlal Bajaj Foundation
- **B.** Institute of Constitutional and Parliamentary Studies
- **C.** G. B. Pant Memorial Society  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** R. D. Birla samara Kosh

**Explanation:** The G. B. Pant Memorial Society instituted an award to recognize outstanding parliamentarians, honoring the legacy of Govind Ballabh Pant, a prominent freedom fighter and parliamentarian. Why not the others: (A) Jamanlal Bajaj Foundation is known for awards in social and economic development, not specifically for parliamentarians; (B) Institute of Constitutional and Parliamentary Studies focuses on research and education rather than instituting awards for parliamentarians; (D) R. D. Birla samara Kosh is associated with charitable and social welfare initiatives, not parliamentary awards.

**Q292. What is the population density of Madhya Pradesh?**

- **A.** 202/sq. km
- **B.** 196/sq. km  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 116/sq. km
- **D.** 68/sq. km

**Explanation:** According to the 2011 Census data, Madhya Pradesh has a population density of approximately 196 persons per square kilometer, making it one of the less densely populated states in India relative to its large geographical area. Why not the others: (A) 202/sq. km is slightly higher than the actual recorded density; (C) 116/sq. km significantly underestimates the population density of the state; (D) 68/sq. km is far too low and would reflect only sparsely populated regions, not the state as a whole.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001)*

- **A.** 20 March 1993
- **B.** 11 January 1992  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 1 December 1987
- **D.** 1 June 1998

**Q294. Kemal Ataturk was**

- **A.** the first President of Independent Kenya
- **B.** the founder of modern Turkey  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** revolutionary leader of Soviet Union
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** Kemal Ataturk (1881–1938) was the founder and first President of the modern Turkish Republic, establishing it in 1923 after the collapse of the Ottoman Empire and leading sweeping reforms to modernize Turkey. Why not the others: (A) the first President of Independent Kenya was Jomo Kenyatta, not Ataturk; (C) the revolutionary leader of the Soviet Union was Vladimir Lenin, not Ataturk; (D) is incorrect because option (B) is factually correct.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/007001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/007001)*

- **A.** Run out
- **B.** Bowled
- **C.** LBW  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Catch out

**Explanation:** Anil Kumble trapped Steve Harmison (England) LBW in the Mohali Test to get his 500th wicket.

**Q296. On whom did the U. S. President George W. Bush bestow, in 2002, the U.S.'s highest award for life time achievement in the field of scientific research?**

- **A.** Arun Netravalli  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Dr.Hargoving Kohrana
- **C.** Subramania Chandrasekar
- **D.** Professor C. Radhakrishna Rao

**Explanation:** Arun Netravalli, an Indian-American scientist, received the National Medal of Science from President George W. Bush in 2002, which is the United States' highest scientific honor for lifetime achievement. Why not the others: (B) Dr. Hargoving Khorana, though a Nobel laureate, received his medal earlier (in 1968); (C) Subramania Chandrasekhar, also a Nobel laureate, passed away in 1995 and his honors were from an earlier era; (D) Professor C. Radhakrishna Rao, while a renowned statistician, did not receive this specific 2002 award from Bush.

**Q297. Saraswathi Samman is given annually for outstanding contribution to**

- **A.** classical music
- **B.** education
- **C.** literature  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** fine arts

**Explanation:** The Saraswathi Samman is a prestigious annual award instituted by the Sangam Literature Foundation that honors outstanding contributions to literature and literary arts across Indian languages. This award recognizes exceptional achievements by writers, poets, and literary scholars. Why not the others: (A) classical music is recognized by other national awards, not the Saraswathi Samman; (B) education contributions are honored through different awards and institutions; (D) fine arts achievements are typically recognized through separate cultural awards.

**Q298. In which year, terrorists crash two planes into New York's World Trade Centre on September 11 in a sequence of destruction?**

- **A.** 2000
- **B.** 2001  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 2002
- **D.** 2003

**Explanation:** The terrorist attacks on the World Trade Centre in New York occurred on September 11, 2001, when al-Qaeda operatives hijacked two commercial airplanes and crashed them into the Twin Towers, resulting in their collapse and nearly 3,000 deaths. This date is a pivotal moment in modern history. Why not the others: (A) 2000 is the year before the attacks; (C) 2002 is the year after the attacks; (D) 2003 is two years after the attacks.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006002)*

**Q300. Where is the permanent secretariat of the SAARC?**

- **A.** Kathmandu  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** New Delhi
- **C.** Islamabad
- **D.** Colombo

**Explanation:** The permanent secretariat of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) is located in Kathmandu, Nepal, as established when SAARC was founded in 1985. This location was chosen as a neutral ground among the member nations. Why not the others: (B) New Delhi is the capital of India, a major SAARC member, but the secretariat was deliberately placed in a smaller nation to maintain neutrality; (C) Islamabad is Pakistan's capital and similarly would not serve as a neutral headquarters; (D) Colombo is Sri Lanka's capital, which also lacks the neutral status that Kathmandu provides among SAARC members.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001)*

**Q301. Who was the first Indian to win the World Amateur Billiards title?**

- **A.** Geet Sethi
- **B.** Wilson Jones  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Michael Ferreira
- **D.** Manoj Kothari

**Explanation:** Wilson Jones was the first Indian to win the World Amateur Billiards Championship, achieving this historic milestone and establishing India's presence in international billiards competition. Why not the others: (A) Geet Sethi is a renowned Indian billiards player but won titles in other formats, not the World Amateur Billiards Championship first; (C) Michael Ferreira is an accomplished billiards player but did not hold the distinction of being the first Indian to win this particular world title; (D) Manoj Kothari is a notable billiards player but was not the first Indian to achieve this specific world championship victory.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/)*

**Q302. How many Lok Sabha seats belong to Rajasthan?**

- **A.** 32
- **B.** 25  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 30
- **D.** 17

**Explanation:** Rajasthan has 25 Lok Sabha seats, which represent the state in the lower house of Parliament. This allocation is based on the state's population and geographical representation as determined by the delimitation commission. Why not the others: (A) 32 is an incorrect number that does not match Rajasthan's actual seat count; (C) 30 overstates the number of seats allocated to the state; (D) 17 significantly understates Rajasthan's representation in the Lok Sabha.

- **A.** Exceptional service in emergency
- **B.** Bravery on battlefield
- **C.** Outstanding performance in sports  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Exceptional service in slum dwellers

**Explanation:** The Arjuna Award, one of India's highest sporting honors, is bestowed upon athletes for exceptional achievement and outstanding performance in sports at the national and international level. It recognizes sporting excellence and has been awarded since 1961 to celebrate India's finest sportspeople. Why not the others: (A) relates to civilian service awards like Ashoka Chakra; (B) concerns military gallantry awards like Param Vir Chakra; (D) is not an official category for any major national award in India.

- **A.** R.E. Grant Govan  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Dr. Maharajkumar Sir Vijaya Ananda
- **C.** Sikandar Hyat Khan
- **D.** Anthony S. D'Mello

**Explanation:** Govan was president from 1928-29 to 1932-33.

**Q306. The Nobel Prize was first awarded in**

- **A.** 1976
- **B.** 1958
- **C.** 1901  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 1972

**Explanation:** The Nobel Prize was first awarded in 1901, established according to Alfred Nobel's will to recognize outstanding contributions to physics, chemistry, medicine, literature, and peace. Why not the others: (A) 1976 is far too late and represents a period when the prize had already been awarded for decades; (B) 1958 is also decades after the first awards; (D) 1972 is similarly well after the prize's inception in the early 20th century.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004)*

**Q307. The chief purpose of crop rotation is to check the loss of top soil**

- **A.** by water erosion
- **B.** by wind erosion
- **C.** by weathering
- **D.** of its mineral content  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The chief purpose of crop rotation is to maintain and restore soil fertility by preventing the depletion of mineral content (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and other nutrients) that occurs when the same crop is grown repeatedly in the same soil. By rotating crops, different plants extract different minerals and some crops (like legumes) even replenish nitrogen through symbiotic relationships with bacteria. Why not the others: (A) water erosion is prevented primarily through soil conservation measures like terracing and contour plowing, not crop rotation; (B) wind erosion is similarly addressed through physical barriers and mulching rather than crop rotation; (C) weathering is a natural geological process that crop rotation does not specifically address.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001)*

---

**Q308. Sharad Joshi Samman is awarded for**

- **A.** Cartoon
- **B.** Satire and essay  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Trade Union Activity
- **D.** Social Service

**Explanation:** The Sharad Joshi Samman is a prestigious award established to recognize and honor outstanding contributions in the field of satire and essay writing, particularly celebrating works that use wit and critical commentary to address social issues. This award is named after Sharad Joshi, a noted satirist and essayist in Indian literature. Why not the others: (A) Cartoon is a distinct visual art form not the primary focus of this literary award; (C) Trade Union Activity is unrelated to Sharad Joshi's literary legacy and achievements; (D) Social Service, while a worthy cause, is too broad and not the specific purpose of this literary honor.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030006](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030006)*

- **A.** Rajiv Gandhi  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Dr. Rajendra Prasad
- **C.** Indira Gandhi
- **D.** Acharya

**Explanation:** The Manav Seva Award was instituted in memory of Rajiv Gandhi, the former Prime Minister of India, to recognize and honor individuals who have made outstanding contributions to humanitarian and social welfare work. Why not the others: (B) Dr. Rajendra Prasad was India's first President, but the Manav Seva Award is specifically associated with Rajiv Gandhi's legacy; (C) Indira Gandhi, while a prominent Prime Minister, is not the person for whom this particular award was instituted; (D) 'Acharya' is incomplete and does not refer to the specific person memorialized by this award.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030009](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030009)*

**Q311. First human heart transplant operation conducted by Dr. Christiaan Barnard on Louis Washkansky, was conducted in**

- **A.** 1967  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 1968
- **C.** 1958
- **D.** 1922

**Explanation:** Louis Washkansky (1913 - 21 December 1967) was the recipient of the world's first human heart transplant. Washkansky died of double pneumonia eighteen days after the transplant because of a weakened immune system.

**Q312. In UNO, the new members are admitted to the General Assembly on the recommendation of ____ and ____ the members of the General Assembly should vote in favour.**

- **A.** Security Council; two-thirds  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Security Council; one-third
- **C.** International Court of Justice; two-thirds
- **D.** International Court of Justice; one-third

**Explanation:** According to the UN Charter, the Security Council must recommend a candidate for UN membership, and the General Assembly must then approve that recommendation by a two-thirds majority vote. This two-thirds requirement ensures substantial consensus among member states for admitting new members. Why not the others: (B) one-third is far below the required supermajority threshold for such an important decision; (C) the International Court of Justice has no role in UN membership admissions—only the Security Council can make such recommendations; (D) the Court is not involved in membership decisions, and one-third does not meet the necessary voting requirement.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006015)*

**Q313. In the match between India and Pakistan at Jaipur on 02-10-1983, Which new rule was introduced?**

- **A.** Limit of overs was reduced to 50 overs
- **B.** The rule of field restriction was taken.
- **C.** Over throw runs were batsman's score
- **D.** No-balls & wides were debited to bowlers analysis  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Before this match, no balls & wides were debited in extras.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003)*

**Q314. Who was named India's Cricketer of the Century by Wisden?**

- **A.** Anil Kumble
- **B.** Sachin Tendulkar
- **C.** Kapil Dev  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Sunil Gavaskar

**Explanation:** Well life can churn some amazing ironies - some years ago, Kapil was charged for MATCH -FIXING ! (and exonerated).

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013002)*

**Q315. Ben Johnson, the well known athlete belongs to which of the following countries?**

- **A.** South Africa
- **B.** Canada  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Germany
- **D.** Cuba

**Explanation:** Ben Johnson was a Canadian sprinter who became famous in the 1980s as one of the fastest runners in the world, competing for Canada in international athletics competitions including the Olympic Games. Why not the others: (A) South Africa has produced notable athletes but Ben Johnson was not among them; (C) Germany has strong athletics traditions but Ben Johnson competed for Canada, not Germany; (D) Cuba has contributed many athletes to world sports but Ben Johnson was not Cuban.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028002)*

**Q316. Theory of Idealism was expounded by George Hegal, Bishop George and Berkeley According to this**

- **A.** mathli is an illusion and that the only reality that which exists mentally  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** good was pleasure and that evil was pain
- **C.** base of knowledge is sense-experience, that is, observations governed by scientific principles
- **D.** all knowledge is derived from sensory experience, by observing and experimenting

**Explanation:** Idealism, as expounded by Hegel, Berkeley, and Bishop George, is a philosophical doctrine asserting that reality is fundamentally mental or mind-dependent, and that the material world as we perceive it is ultimately an illusion—only mental phenomena constitute true reality. Option (A) correctly captures this core principle of idealism. Why not the others: (B) describes hedonism (a theory about pleasure and pain), not idealism; (C) describes empiricism based on sense-experience, which contradicts idealism's rejection of materialism; (D) similarly represents empiricism, which emphasizes sensory observation over mental reality.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001)*

---

**Q317. The first recipient of Rajiv Gandhi's 'Khel Ratna' award is**

- **A.** Vishwanathan Anand  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Leander Peas
- **C.** Kapil Dev
- **D.** Limba Ram

**Explanation:** Vishwanathan Anand, the renowned chess grandmaster, was the first recipient of the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna award (now known as the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award) when it was instituted in 1991-92. His exceptional contribution to Indian chess and his world-class performance made him the inaugural awardee of this prestigious national sports honor. Why not the others: (B) Leander Peas was a tennis player who received the award in later years, not first; (C) Kapil Dev, though a cricket legend, received the award after Anand; (D) Limba Ram was an archer but was not the first recipient of this award.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030002)*

---

**Q318. The number of already named bones in the human skeleton is**

- **A.** 200
- **B.** 206  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 212
- **D.** 218

**Explanation:** An adult human skeleton contains 206 bones, which is the standard anatomical count used in medical and biological sciences. This is the established number of named bones in a fully developed human skeleton after the fusion of certain bones that occurs during growth. Why not the others: (A) 200 is lower than the actual count of distinct named bones; (C) 212 and (D) 218 both exceed the correct anatomical number, possibly confusing the total with bone counts in infants (who have more separate bones due to unfused cartilage) or misremembering the standard figure.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001)*

---

**Q319. The 'Black flag' signifies**

- **A.** revolution/danger
- **B.** peace
- **C.** protest  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** truce

**Explanation:** The black flag historically and conventionally signifies protest, dissent, or disapproval—it is commonly used in demonstrations, strikes, and civil disobedience to express opposition to policies or actions. Why not the others: (A) while black may evoke danger in some contexts, it is not the standard symbolic meaning of the flag itself; (B) peace is typically represented by white flags or doves, not black; (D) a truce is signified by a white flag, which is the opposite of black in this symbolic context.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001)*

- **B.** M. F. Hussain  ✅ **(correct answer)**

- **D.** V. K. Gokak

Hussain, the renowned painter and filmmaker, was not a recipient of the Bharatiya Jnanpith Award, which is India's highest literary honor given to authors for their outstanding contributions to literature. Why not the others: (A) Ashaparna Devi was a recipient of the award for her contributions to Bengali literature; (C) Raghupathi Sahai was honored for his literary work; (D) V. K. Gokak received the award for his contributions to Kannada literature and literary scholarship.

- **A.** M.L.Valsamma
- **B.** P.T.Usha
- **C.** Kamaljit Sandhu  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** K.Malleshwari

**Explanation:** Kamaljit Sandhu was the first Indian woman to win an Asian Games gold medal in the 400m run event, achieving this historic feat at the 1982 Asian Games held in Delhi. She broke the barrier for Indian women in this specific track and field discipline at the continental level. Why not the others: (A) M.L.Valsamma was a notable distance runner but did not win an Asian Games gold in 400m; (B) P.T.Usha, though a legendary sprinter and hurdler, won medals in 400m hurdles and relays, not the open 400m race; (D) K.Malleshwari is an accomplished weightlifter, not a runner.

- **A.** 1992
- **B.** 1993
- **C.** 1994  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 1995

**Explanation:** Kiran Bedi, a pioneering Indian police officer and social activist, received the Ramon Magsaysay Award for Government Service in 1994 in recognition of her exceptional work in police reform and community service. Why not the others: (A) 1992 is too early for the timing of her most significant reform initiatives that led to the award; (B) 1993 predates the actual year of the award; (D) 1995 is after the correct year of recognition.

**Q323. The chief constituent of gobar gas is**

- **A.** ethane
- **B.** methane  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** hydrogen
- **D.** carbon dioxide

**Explanation:** Gobar gas, produced through anaerobic digestion of cattle dung and organic waste, has methane as its primary constituent, typically comprising 50-70% of the gas mixture. Methane is the main combustible component that makes gobar gas useful as a fuel source. Why not the others: (A) ethane is present only in trace amounts in gobar gas; (C) hydrogen is not a significant component of biogas produced from anaerobic digestion; (D) while carbon dioxide is a major component of gobar gas (20-40%), it is not the chief constituent by composition or functional importance.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001)*

**Q324. Name the Indian Chess player who has broken the World Record by finishing the game in first twelve moves?**

- **A.** Vishwanathan Anand  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Champa Bose
- **C.** Sakunthala Devi
- **D.** Praveen Thipsey

**Explanation:** Vishwanathan Anand holds a notable distinction in chess history for completing a game in an exceptionally brief number of moves, demonstrating his mastery of opening theory and tactical prowess. This record reflects his achievement in one of chess's fastest decisive games played at a competitive level. Why not the others: (B) Champa Bose was a pioneering Indian chess player but is not known for this specific world record; (C) Sakunthala Devi was famous for mental arithmetic and memory feats rather than chess records; (D) Praveen Thipsey is not a recognized figure associated with this chess milestone.

- **D.** Dr. H. J. Bhabha

Why not the others: (A) J.C. Bose was a pioneering Indian physicist but did not win a Nobel Prize; (C) Vickram Sarabhai was an eminent Indian scientist and space program founder but did not win the Nobel Prize in Physics; (D) H. J. Bhabha was a brilliant nuclear physicist who contributed significantly to India's atomic program but did not win the Nobel Prize in Physics.

- **C.** 9 - 1 - 38 - 3

**Q328. The Olympic Flame, was, for the first time, ceremonially lighted and burnt in a giant torch at the entrance of the stadium at**

- **A.** Athens Games (1896)
- **B.** London Games (1908)
- **C.** Paris Games (1924)
- **D.** Amsterdam Games (1928)  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The Olympic Flame was first ceremonially lit and burned in a giant torch at the stadium entrance during the 1928 Amsterdam Games, establishing a tradition that continues at all subsequent Olympic ceremonies. This symbolic practice of the Olympic Flame burning throughout the Games began in Amsterdam and has become one of the most iconic elements of the Olympic opening ceremony. Why not the others: (A) Athens 1896 was the first modern Olympics but did not feature the ceremonial Olympic Flame tradition; (B) London 1908 also predates the establishment of this ceremony; (C) Paris 1924 came before Amsterdam but did not introduce the Olympic Flame ceremony.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001)*

**Q329. Excessive secretion from the pituitary gland in the children results in**

- **A.** increased height  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** retarded growth
- **C.** weakening of bones
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** Excessive secretion of growth hormone from the anterior pituitary gland during childhood results in increased height because growth hormone directly stimulates bone and tissue growth, leading to taller stature and gigantism if unchecked. Why not the others: (B) retarded growth occurs with insufficient, not excessive, pituitary secretion; (C) weakening of bones is not a primary direct effect of excessive growth hormone secretion; (D) incorrect because option (A) correctly describes the outcome of excessive pituitary secretion in children.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005016](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005016)*

- **A.** Labour Party
- **B.** Nazi Party  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Ku-Klux-Klan
- **D.** Democratic Party

This is a fundamental historical fact about the rise of fascism in Europe. Why not the others: (A) The Labour Party is a political party in the United Kingdom, not Hitler's party in Germany; (C) The Ku-Klux-Klan is a racist organization from the United States, not a German political party; (D) The Democratic Party is associated with politics in the United States, not Hitler's regime.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005002)*

**Q331. Which is the highest gallantry award in India?**

- **A.** Param Vishishtat Seva Medal
- **B.** Param Vir Chakra  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Kirti Chakra
- **D.** Vir Chakra

**Explanation:** The Param Vir Chakra (PVC) is India's highest military decoration awarded for the highest degree of valour or self-sacrifice in the presence of the enemy, similar to the British Victoria Cross, US Medal of Honor, Pakistani Nishan-e-Haider, or French Legion of Honor or Russian Cross of St. George.

- **B.** Physics

The original Nobel Prizes established in 1901 included Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, Literature, and Peace—but Economics was not among them. **Q337. The main objectives of the UN are**

- **A.** to maintain peace and security in the world
- **B.** to work together to remove poverty, disease and illiteracy and encourage respect for each other's rights of basic freedom.
- **C.** to develop friendly relations among nations
- **D.** All of the above  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The United Nations Charter explicitly outlines four main purposes, and option (D) correctly identifies that all of the listed objectives are indeed primary goals of the UN. Maintaining international peace and security, promoting human rights and freedoms, addressing socioeconomic challenges, and fostering friendly relations among nations are all foundational objectives of the organization. Why not the others: (A) addresses only one pillar of the UN's mission while ignoring humanitarian and developmental goals; (B) focuses narrowly on social and economic development while omitting the critical peace and security mandate; (C) emphasizes international relations but excludes security, human rights, and development objectives.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001)*

**Q338. Former Australian captain Mark Taylor has had several nicknames over his playing career. Which of the following was NOT one of them?**

- **A.** Tubby
- **B.** Stodge
- **C.** Helium Bat
- **D.** Stumpy  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** He was christened 'Helium Bat' very early in his career by team mates, due to his habit of lifting his bat over his shoulder and letting anything bowled outside the off-stump go by.

- **A.** UNESCO for exceptional contribution of efforts to popularise science  ✅ **(correct answer)**

- **C.** Jnanpith, a cultural literacy society for encouraging creative writing in modern languages
- **D.** Indian Adult Education Association for outstanding contribution of adult literacy in India

**Explanation:** The Kalinga Award was instituted by UNESCO in 1952 to recognize exceptional contributions to the popularization of science, making option (A) correct. Why not the others: (B) The Indian Military does not institute awards for science popularization; (C) Jnanpith gives the Jnanpith Award for creative writing, not the Kalinga Award; (D) The Indian Adult Education Association focuses on adult literacy programs, not science promotion through the Kalinga Award.

- **A.** 1
- **B.** 2
- **C.** 3
- **D.** 4  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** P.T. Usha, the legendary Indian athlete, won 4 gold medals at the 1986 Seoul Asian Games, cementing her status as one of Asia's finest track and field performers of that era. Why not the others: (A) 1 gold medal significantly undercounts her dominant performance at those games; (B) 2 gold medals falls short of her actual medal haul; (C) 3 gold medals is still below her actual achievement of 4 medals.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012015)*

**Q341. When light passes from air into glass it experiences change of**

- **A.** frequency and wavelength
- **B.** frequency and speed
- **C.** wavelength and speed  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** frequency, wavelength and speed

**Explanation:** When light enters a denser medium like glass from air, its speed decreases due to the medium's refractive index, and consequently its wavelength also decreases (since wavelength = speed/frequency). However, the frequency of light remains constant because it is determined by the light source and does not change when light enters a different medium. Why not the others: (A) incorrectly includes frequency as changing; (B) incorrectly includes frequency as changing; (D) incorrectly suggests that frequency changes, which it does not.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001)*

**Q342. Which of the following is a pair names of the same game?**

- **A.** Soccer - Football  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Golf - Polo
- **C.** Billiards - Carrom
- **D.** Volleyball - Squash

**Explanation:** Soccer and Football are two names for the same sport — a team sport played with a ball between two goals where players primarily use their feet to control and advance the ball. Why not the others: (B) Golf and Polo are completely different sports with different equipment, playing surfaces, and objectives; (C) Billiards and Carrom are distinct games with different rules, tables, and gameplay mechanics; (D) Volleyball and Squash are separate sports played in different formats and with different equipment and court configurations.

C. Roy Award, which is exclusively medical in nature.

- **A.** largest railway station  ✅ **(correct answer)**

- **C.** longest railway station
- **D.** None of the above

- **D.** Chetan Chauhan

This remarkable partnership between two Indian opening batsmen remains one of the most significant achievements in Test cricket's early era. Why not the others: (A) Subhash Gupte was a bowler, not an opening batsman; (B) Sunil Gavaskar, though a great batsman, did not form this historic partnership with Mankad; (D) Chetan Chauhan was a later-generation opener and not part of this record partnership.

- **B.** Fifteen
- **C.** Twenty one  ✅ **(correct answer)**

This structure ensures diverse geographical and political representation across the organization, with representatives from different regional groups and nations sharing responsibility in steering the Assembly's proceedings. Why not the others: (A) Seven is too small a number and does not reflect the actual constitutional structure of the General Assembly; (B) Fifteen is closer but still falls short of the correct number needed for adequate global representation; (D) Two would provide insufficient representation for the diverse membership of the United Nations.

**Q349. Who among the following own the Nobel prize for literature?**

- **A.** Linus Pauling
- **B.** Samuel Beckett  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Philip Noel - Baker
- **D.** Ralph Bunche

**Explanation:** Samuel Beckett, the Irish author and playwright, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1969 for his innovative contributions to drama and fiction, particularly works like 'Waiting for Godot.' Why not the others: (A) Linus Pauling won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1954) and the Nobel Peace Prize (1962), not Literature; (C) Philip Noel-Baker received the Nobel Peace Prize (1959), not Literature; (D) Ralph Bunche was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize (1950), not Literature.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004)*

---

**Q350. In which year of First World War Germany declared war on Russia and France?**

- **A.** 1914  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 1915
- **C.** 1916
- **D.** 1917

**Explanation:** Germany declared war on Russia on August 1, 1914, and on France on August 3, 1914, early in the First World War after Russia's mobilization in response to the Austro-Serbian crisis. Why not the others: (B) 1915 — by this year Germany was already engaged in war with both nations; (C) 1916 — this was well into the conflict, not when declarations were made; (D) 1917 — Germany had been at war with these nations for years by this point.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006001)*

---

**Q351. The first hang gliders to be flown were flown in...?**

- **A.** 1600s
- **B.** 1700s
- **C.** 1800s  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 1900s

**Explanation:** The first successful hang gliders were developed and flown in the 1800s, with pioneers like Otto Lilienthal conducting systematic experiments with gliding aircraft in Germany during the late 19th century. Why not the others: (A) the 1600s predates any practical hang gliding development; (B) the 1700s saw some theoretical work but no successful flights; (D) the 1900s came after the first hang gliders had already been flown.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003)*

---

**Q352. Squadron leader Rakesh Sharma was India's first man to go into space. He was ____ cosmonaut to be in space in the world.**

- **A.** 12th
- **B.** 107th
- **C.** 139th  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 151st

**Explanation:** Squadron Leader Rakesh Sharma became India's first astronaut when he flew aboard Soyuz T-11 to the Salyut 7 space station in April 1984, making him the 139th person overall to reach space. This count includes all cosmonauts and astronauts from various nations who had reached space by that date. Why not the others: (A) 12th is far too low and would place him among the very earliest space travelers from the 1960s; (B) 107th does not align with the historical records of space missions up to 1984; (D) 151st exceeds the actual number of people who had reached space by the time of his mission.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001)*

---

**Q353. Which of the following awards was conferred upon Smt. Aruna Asaf Ali for her role in the welfare of adult and women?**

- **A.** Tagore Literacy Award
- **B.** UNSECO Literacy Award
- **C.** Moorti Devi Award  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Kalinga Prize

**Explanation:** Smt. Aruna Asaf Ali was conferred the Moorti Devi Award for her significant contributions to the welfare of adults and women, particularly in literacy and social development. Why not the others: (A) the Tagore Literacy Award is given for contributions to literacy but Aruna Asaf Ali received the Moorti Devi Award specifically; (B) the UNESCO Literacy Award is an international award and not the award she received for this work; (D) the Kalinga Prize is awarded for science communication and popular science writing, not for welfare activities.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007)*

---

**Q354. Which countries from the membership of CACM (Central American Common Market)?**

- **A.** Costa Rica, EI Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Bogota, EI Salvedor, Honduras
- **C.** Washington DC, Bogota, Guatemala
- **D.** All of the above

**Explanation:** The Central American Common Market (CACM) is a regional trade bloc comprising five Central American nations: Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, and Nicaragua. These countries established the organization to promote economic integration and free trade among themselves. Why not the others: (B) includes Bogota (a city, not a country) and omits Costa Rica and Nicaragua, which are actual CACM members; (C) lists Washington DC and Bogota (neither countries nor CACM members) instead of the correct member states; (D) is incorrect because options B and C are factually wrong and cannot be correct alternatives.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001)*

---

## Current Affairs — Standard 7

**Q355. The treaty which ushered in NATO, was signed by the member nations at**

- **A.** Geneva
- **B.** London
- **C.** Paris
- **D.** Washington  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The North Atlantic Treaty, which formally established NATO, was signed in Washington D.C. on April 4, 1949, by the founding member nations. This treaty created the military and political alliance that became one of the most significant geopolitical institutions of the post-World War II era. Why not the others: (A) Geneva is home to many international organizations but was not the signing location of the NATO founding treaty; (B) London hosted various post-war conferences but not the NATO treaty signing; (C) Paris was a significant European diplomatic center but did not host the formal NATO treaty signing ceremony.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001)*

---

**Q356. What was Sachin Tendulkar's average when he made his first thousand runs in ODIs?**

- **A.** 35.83  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 22.92
- **C.** 43.87
- **D.** 42.86

**Explanation:** He got his 1000th run against Zimbabawe in '92

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013002)*

---

**Q357. Philology is the**

- **A.** study of bones
- **B.** study of muscles
- **C.** study of architecture
- **D.** science of languages  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Philology is the science of languages, encompassing the study of language origins, development, historical relationships, and textual analysis across different periods and cultures. Why not the others: (A) study of bones is osteology; (B) study of muscles is myology; (C) study of architecture is architectural history or architecture itself, not philology.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/008001)*

---

**Q358. The UNSECO's 'Prix Jules Verne' prize has been given to which of the following serials of Doordarshan?**

- **A.** The World This Week
- **B.** Surabhi
- **C.** Eye Witness
- **D.** Turning Point  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** Doordarshan's 'Turning Point' serial received UNESCO's Prix Jules Verne award, recognizing its excellence in educational and scientific programming that aligns with Jules Verne's spirit of exploration and discovery. Why not the others: (A) The World This Week is a news-focused program, not the recipient of this specific UNESCO prize; (B) Surabhi is a cultural and arts program that did not win the Prix Jules Verne; (C) Eye Witness is a documentary-style series that, while informative, was not the UNESCO award recipient.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030005](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030005)*

---

**Q359. Who has scored the most runs in a single First-class innings?**

- **A.** Hanif Mohammad
- **B.** Brian Lara  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Sir Donald Bradman
- **D.** Sachin Tendulkar

**Explanation:** Brian Lara holds the record for the most runs scored in a single First-class innings with 501 not out, which he achieved in 1994 while playing for Warwickshire. This remains an unbeaten First-class record and is one of cricket's most celebrated individual performances. Why not the others: (A) Hanif Mohammad scored 499, which is the second-highest First-class innings score; (C) Sir Donald Bradman, despite his legendary batting average, never scored a First-class double-century in excess of this record; (D) Sachin Tendulkar's highest First-class innings is well below 501 runs.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012016](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012016)*

---

**Q360. The countries that had maintained research stations in Antarctica under Antarctic Trade are**

- **A.** Argentina, Australia, Belgium and South Africa
- **B.** Chili, France and Japan
- **C.** New Zealand, Norway, the former USSR, the UK and the USA
- **D.** All of the above  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The Antarctic Treaty (1959) established Antarctica as a scientific preserve and allowed multiple nations to maintain research stations there. Under the treaty's framework, numerous countries including Argentina, Australia, Belgium, South Africa, Chile, France, Japan, New Zealand, Norway, the former USSR, the UK, and the USA all established and operated research stations on the continent, making option (D) the correct answer. Why not the others: (A) lists only some of the treaty nations with stations, omitting major ones like Chile, France, and the USA; (B) similarly includes only three countries while excluding many others like Argentina, Australia, and the UK; (C) is incomplete as it excludes important station-operating nations like Argentina, Australia, Belgium, Chile, France, Japan, and South Africa.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/019001)*

---

**Q361. The 'Cannes Award' is given for excellence in which field?**

- **A.** Films  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Journalism
- **C.** Literature
- **D.** Economics

**Explanation:** The Cannes Award, officially the Palme d'Or and other awards from the Cannes Film Festival, is one of the world's most prestigious awards for cinematic achievement, recognizing excellence in filmmaking and cinema. Why not the others: (B) Journalism awards like the Pulitzer Prize exist separately; (C) Literature has the Nobel Prize in Literature and other specific literary awards; (D) Economics has the Nobel Prize in Economics, not Cannes recognition.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030004)*

---

**Q362. Fa-Hien was**

- **A.** the first Buddhist pilgrim of China to visit India during the reign of Chandragupta Vikramaditya  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** the discoverer of Puerto Rico and Jamaica
- **C.** the first Buddhist pilgrim of India to visit China
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** Fa-Hien (also spelled Faxian) was a Chinese Buddhist monk who traveled to India during the early 5th century, during the reign of Chandragupta Vikramaditya of the Gupta Empire, making him the first recorded Buddhist pilgrim from China to visit India. He is famous for his detailed accounts of Indian Buddhism and society during this period. Why not the others: (B) confuses Fa-Hien with European explorers like Columbus; (C) reverses the direction of his pilgrimage—he went from China to India, not the other way; (D) is incorrect because option A accurately describes Fa-Hien's historical significance.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005016](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/005016)*

---

**Q363. The headquarter of ESCAP Economic and Social Commission for Asia are situated at**

- **A.** Bangkok  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Geneva
- **C.** Santiago (Chile)
- **D.** Baghdad

**Explanation:** ESCAP (Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific) is a regional commission of the United Nations for Asia-Pacific, and its headquarters is located in Bangkok, Thailand, making it the correct answer. Why not the others: (B) Geneva hosts the headquarters of several UN organizations but not ESCAP; (C) Santiago is the headquarters of ECLAC (Economic Commission for Latin America and the Caribbean), a different regional commission; (D) Baghdad is not a headquarters location for any major UN regional economic commission.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001)*

**Q364. What is the middle name of Deep Dasgupta?**

- **A.** Karan
- **B.** Jogan
- **C.** Chandar
- **D.** Biplab  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** He was born on 7th June 1977.

**Q365. Which company manufacturers low and high power communication equipment like radius for the use of defence services and paramilitary organisations?**

- **A.** Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL)  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** God Shipyard Limited (GSL)
- **C.** Bharat Dynamic Limited (BDL)
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) is a government-owned defence PSU that manufactures a wide range of electronic equipment including communication systems, radios, and other low and high power communication devices for use by defence services and paramilitary organisations. Why not the others: (B) Godrej Shipyard Limited primarily specializes in shipbuilding and ship repair, not communication equipment; (C) Bharat Dynamics Limited focuses on missile systems and related defence production, not communication radios; (D) None of the above is incorrect because BEL is indeed the correct manufacturer of such communication equipment.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/024001)*

**Q366. How many wickets did Yograj Singh take in his 1st ODI match?**

- **A.** 1
- **B.** 2  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 4
- **D.** 3

**Explanation:** Yograj Singh was a bowler, but his son Yuvraj Singh, also nicknamed as Yuvi, turned out to be a smashing batsman.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/013001)*

**Q367. 'Global 500' awards are given for the outstanding achievement in which of the following fields?**

- **A.** Campaign against AIDS
- **B.** Population control
- **C.** Protection of environment  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Elimination of illiteracy

**Explanation:** The 'Global 500' awards are presented by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) to individuals and organizations who have made outstanding contributions to environmental protection and conservation. This prestigious award recognizes exemplary work in safeguarding ecosystems, wildlife, and natural resources worldwide. Why not the others: (A) AIDS awareness campaigns are recognized through different international health awards, not the Global 500 program; (B) population control initiatives, while important, are not the focus of the Global 500 awards; (D) literacy programs are acknowledged through education-focused awards and UNESCO initiatives, not this environmental award.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030010](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030010)*

**Q368. Which of the following films has won 9 Oscar Awards in 1987?**

- **A.** The World Safari
- **B.** The Last Emperor  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Rain Man
- **D.** Amadeus

**Explanation:** The Last Emperor, directed by Bernardo Bertolucci, won 9 Academy Awards at the 1987 Oscar ceremony (for 1986 films), making it one of the most awarded films in a single year. Why not the others: (A) The World Safari is not a major Oscar-winning film of that era; (C) Rain Man won 4 Oscars in 1989, not 9 in 1987; (D) Amadeus won 11 Oscars in 1985, not 9 in 1987.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030009](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030009)*

**Q369. On whose name is the highest award for services to the development of cinema given?**

- **A.** Raj Kapoor
- **B.** Dada Saheb  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Meena Kumari
- **D.** Amitabh Bachchan

**Explanation:** The highest award for services to the development of cinema in India is the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, named after Dada Saheb Phalke, the pioneering filmmaker and founder of Indian cinema who created the first full-length Indian feature film. It is a prestigious national honor given annually to recognize lifetime contributions to Indian cinema. Why not the others: (A) Raj Kapoor was an influential actor and filmmaker but the award is not named after him; (C) Meena Kumari was a talented actress but not the namesake of cinema's highest award; (D) Amitabh Bachchan is a renowned actor but the award predates his prominence and is not named after him.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030007)*

---

**Q370. Who among the following is a winner of the Indira Gandhi Award for National integration?**

- **A.** Aruna Asaf Ali  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Ashok Kumar
- **C.** Sundarlal Bahuguna
- **D.** M. G. K. Menon

**Explanation:** Aruna Asaf Ali was a freedom fighter and social activist who received the Indira Gandhi Award for National Integration in recognition of her significant contributions to national integration and social causes throughout her life. Why not the others: (B) Ashok Kumar was a renowned actor known for cinema, not primarily recognized with this national integration award; (C) Sundarlal Bahuguna was an environmental activist focused on the Chipko movement, not a recipient of this specific award; (D) M. G. K. Menon was a scientist and administrator whose recognition lay primarily in scientific and educational spheres rather than this award.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030008](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030008)*

**Q371. The highest civilian award of India 'Bharat Ratna' has been awarded to only two foreigners so far. One of them is Nelson Mandela. The other is**

- **A.** Abdul Ghaffar khan  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Mikhail Gorbachev
- **C.** Marshal Tito
- **D.** Abdul Wali Khan

**Explanation:** Abdul Ghaffar Khan, known as 'Badshah Khan' or the 'Frontier Gandhi,' is the only other foreigner besides Nelson Mandela to have been awarded the Bharat Ratna. He received this highest civilian award in 1987 in recognition of his role in the Indian independence movement and his advocacy of non-violence. Why not the others: (B) Mikhail Gorbachev was a Soviet leader but never received the Bharat Ratna; (C) Marshal Tito was a Yugoslav leader and was not awarded this honour; (D) Abdul Wali Khan was an Afghan/Pakistani political figure but did not receive the Bharat Ratna.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030006](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030006)*

**Q372. ICAO stands for**

- **A.** International Civil Aviation Organization  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Indian Corporation of Agriculture Organization
- **C.** Institute of Company of Accounts Organization
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations established to oversee the safe and orderly development of international civil aviation. It sets international standards and recommended practices for aviation safety, security, efficiency, and environmental protection. Why not the others: (B) refers to an incorrect acronym unrelated to aviation; (C) is not what ICAO stands for and confuses it with accounting bodies; (D) is incorrect since option (A) is the verified and standard definition.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006001)*

**Q373. Who holds second and third place for most yards rushing in a single season in the NFL?**

- **A.** Barry Sanders
- **B.** Jim Brown
- **C.** OJ Simpson  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Dick Lane

**Explanation:** OJ Simpson holds the second-place record for most rushing yards in a single NFL season with 2,003 yards in 1973, and also holds third place with 1,862 yards in 1972, making him the only player to occupy both the second and third positions on this list. Why not the others: (A) Barry Sanders holds the first-place record, not second or third; (B) Jim Brown, while a legendary rusher, does not hold second or third place in single-season rushing yards; (D) Dick Lane was primarily a defensive back and cornerback, not a running back, so he would not appear on rushing yards records.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012015)*

**Q374. Which was the 1st non Test playing country to beat India in an international match?**

- **A.** Canada
- **B.** Sri Lanka  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Zimbabwe
- **D.** East Africa

**Explanation:** Sri Lanka got the status of Test playing country in 1981, and beat India in the 1979 World Cup. Before this they were champion of ICC non-test playing countries.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/)*

**Q375. The Olympic Flame symbolises**

- **A.** unity among various nations of the world
- **B.** speed, perfection and strength
- **C.** sports as a means for securing harmony among nations
- **D.** continuity between the ancient and modern games  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The Olympic Flame is historically rooted in ancient Greece, where it burned continuously during the ancient Olympic Games as a sacred symbol. Its presence at modern Olympic Games serves as a direct link connecting today's global sporting event to its ancient origins, representing the unbroken tradition and continuity between ancient and modern Olympic competitions. Why not the others: (A) while the Olympics do unite nations, this is not what the flame itself symbolizes; (B) speed, perfection, and strength are Olympic values but not the specific meaning of the flame; (C) harmony among nations is an outcome or purpose of the Games, not the symbolic meaning of the flame itself.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001)*

**Q376. When is the World's Diabetes Day?**

- **A.** 14th November  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 11th December
- **C.** 15th October
- **D.** 1st July

**Explanation:** World's Diabetes Day is observed on 14th November every year, commemorating the birth anniversary of Sir Frederick Banting, who discovered insulin and played a crucial role in diabetes research. Why not the others: (B) 11th December is observed as International Mountain Day; (C) 15th October is observed as Global Handwashing Day; (D) 1st July marks the founding of various organizations but is not World Diabetes Day.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/023001)*

- **B.** Northamptonshire & Warwickshire
- **C.** Nottinghamshire & Worcestershire
- **D.** Nottinghamshire & Warwickshire

**Explanation:** Kapil Dev, the legendary Indian cricketer and former captain, played county cricket for Northamptonshire and Worcestershire during his playing career in England. Why not the others: (B) Warwickshire was not one of the counties where Kapil Dev played; (C) Nottinghamshire was not among the counties associated with his county cricket stint; (D) neither Nottinghamshire nor Warwickshire were the counties where Kapil Dev played his county cricket.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012002)*

- **A.** Australia
- **B.** Canada
- **C.** Kenya  ✅ **(correct answer)**

Why not the others: (A) Australia has not produced a world record holder for 10,000 meters; (B) Canada's strength is in different running distances and events; (D) Mexico's distance running achievements are not at the level of holding 10,000-meter world records.

**Q379. What is the middle name of Sourav Ganguly?**

- **A.** Chandidas  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Mohandas
- **C.** Chandradas
- **D.** Tarwadi

**Explanation:** The Prince was born on 8th July 1972.

**Q380. Which state gives Mewar Award?**

- **A.** Haryana
- **B.** Delhi
- **C.** Punjab
- **D.** Rajasthan  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Explanation:** The Mewar Award is a prestigious award given by the state of Rajasthan to recognize outstanding contributions in various fields including culture, education, and social service. This award is deeply connected to the Mewar region's historical and cultural heritage, which is located in Rajasthan. Why not the others: (A) Haryana has its own distinct awards and is not associated with the Mewar legacy; (B) Delhi, being the national capital, gives national-level awards but not the Mewar Award; (C) Punjab does not administer the Mewar Award, which is specifically tied to Rajasthan's regional identity.

**Q381. 'Popping Crease' is associated with which games?**

- **A.** Swimming
- **B.** Cricket  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Water polo
- **D.** Lawn Tennis

**Explanation:** The odd name of the popping crease refers to the early history of the game of cricket, when batsmen used to have to 'pop' their bats into a small hole that was located in the middle of the crease for a run to count. For a player to run a batsman out, he had to pop the ball into the hole before the bat was grounded in it.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/028002)*

**Q382. FFC stands for**

- **A.** Foreign Finance Corporation
- **B.** Film Finance Corporation  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Federation of Football Council
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** FFC stands for Film Finance Corporation, which is a financial institution in India established to support and finance film production, particularly in the Indian cinema industry. Why not the others: (A) Foreign Finance Corporation is not what FFC represents; (C) Federation of Football Council is unrelated to FFC's actual function in the film industry; (D) None of the above is incorrect since option B correctly identifies FFC.

**Q383. Entomology is the science that studies**

- **A.** Behavior of human beings
- **B.** Insects  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** The origin and history of technical and scientific terms
- **D.** The formation of rocks

**Explanation:** Entomology: The branch of zoology concerned with the study of insects.

**Q384. In which of the following years, the membership of the Security Council was increased from 11 to 15 (under Article 23)?**

- **A.** 1960
- **B.** 1965  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 1972
- **D.** 1975

**Explanation:** The Security Council's membership was expanded from 11 to 15 permanent and non-permanent members in 1965 through an amendment to Article 23 of the UN Charter. This expansion increased the number of non-permanent members from six to ten while keeping the five permanent members unchanged, reflecting the growing number of UN member states after decolonization. Why not the others: (A) 1960 was the year of significant African decolonization but not when the Security Council expansion occurred; (C) 1972 is too late and marks no relevant Security Council change; (D) 1975 is also too late and not the year of the membership increase.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006002)*

---

**Q385. Which two counties did Anil Kumble play for?**

- **A.** Nottinghamshire & Lancashire
- **B.** Nottinghamshire & Leicestershire
- **C.** Northamptonshire & Leicestershire  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Northamptonshire & Lancashire

**Explanation:** Kumble had a great season with Northants taking 105 wickets.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012003)*

**Q386. Susi Susanti, held the Grand Slam title in which of the following games**

- **A.** Lawn Tennis
- **B.** Tennis
- **C.** Badminton  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** volley ball

**Explanation:** Susi Susanti was an Indonesian badminton player who won the Grand Slam title in badminton, making option (C) correct. She is particularly known for her achievements in the sport during the 1990s. Why not the others: (A) Lawn Tennis refers specifically to grass-court tennis, and Susanti was not a tennis player; (B) Tennis is too broad and Susanti was not a professional tennis competitor; (D) Volleyball is a completely different sport in which Susanti had no Grand Slam achievements.

**Q387. Logarithm tables were invented by**

- **A.** John Napier  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** John Doe
- **C.** John Harrison
- **D.** John Douglas

**Explanation:** John Napier, a Scottish mathematician, invented logarithms in the early 17th century, publishing his work 'Mirifici Logarithmorum Canonis Descriptio' in 1614, which revolutionized mathematical calculations by reducing complex multiplication and division to simpler addition and subtraction operations. Why not the others: (B) John Doe is a generic placeholder name with no historical mathematical significance; (C) John Harrison was a clockmaker famous for solving the longitude problem, not mathematics; (D) John Douglas is not associated with any major mathematical invention or discovery.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/007001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/007001)*

**Q388. The Arjuna Awards were instituted in the year**

- **A.** 1965
- **B.** 1961  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 1963
- **D.** 1957

**Explanation:** The Arjuna Awards, India's second-highest sporting honor, were instituted in 1961 to recognize outstanding achievement and sportsmanship among Indian athletes. Why not the others: (A) 1965 is too late and post-dates the actual establishment; (C) 1963 is also after the correct year; (D) 1957 is before the awards were created.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030003](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/030003)*

**Q389. In which year was Pulitzer Prize established?**

- **A.** 1917  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 1918
- **C.** 1922
- **D.** 1928

**Explanation:** The Pulitzer Prize was established in 1917, following Joseph Pulitzer's bequest and vision to recognize excellence in journalism, letters, drama, and music in the United States. Why not the others: (B) 1918 is one year after the actual establishment and may confuse the first awards ceremony with the founding; (C) 1922 and (D) 1928 are both significantly later years that do not correspond to when the prize was formally instituted.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/honours-and-awards/)*

**Q390. The 2006 World Cup Football Tournament held in**

- **A.** France
- **B.** China
- **C.** Germany  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Brazil

- **C.** V. Shantaram
- **D.** Ashok Kumar

**Explanation:** Ramanand Sagar, though a legendary filmmaker and television producer known for creating the iconic Ramayan series, was not a recipient of the Dada Saheb Phalke Award, which is India's highest award for cinema. Why not the others: (B) Raj Kapoor was a pioneering actor-director-producer who received the award in recognition of his immense contribution to Indian cinema; (C) V. Shantaram was an accomplished filmmaker who was honored with the Dada Saheb Phalke Award for his significant work in Indian film industry; (D) Ashok Kumar, a legendary actor and producer, received the award for his distinguished career spanning decades in Hindi cinema.

**Q392. The first development flight of SLV-3 took place on**

- **A.** May 31, 1981  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** April 17, 1983
- **C.** December 21, 1999
- **D.** December 28, 1995

**Explanation:** SLV-3 (Satellite Launch Vehicle-3) was India's first indigenous satellite launch vehicle, and its first development flight successfully took place on May 31, 1981, marking a major milestone in India's space program. This inaugural flight demonstrated India's capability to design and launch its own satellites independently. Why not the others: (B) April 17, 1983 was the date of SLV-3's second developmental flight, not the first; (C) December 21, 1999 relates to later space missions, not SLV-3's first flight; (D) December 28, 1995 falls outside the period of SLV-3's initial development flights and refers to a different space mission.

- **B.** Calcutta

- **D.** Mumbai  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Q394. In which year during World War I, USA declared war on Germany, Britain defeated Turkey and captured Baghdad and Jerusalem?**

- **A.** 1917  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 1918
- **C.** 1919
- **D.** 1920

**Explanation:** The year 1917 marked three significant World War I events: the United States declared war on Germany in April, Britain defeated the Ottoman Turkish forces and captured Baghdad in March, and British forces captured Jerusalem in December. Why not the others: (B) 1918 was the year the war ended with the armistice in November, not when these major events occurred; (C) 1919 was when the Treaty of Versailles was signed, post-war; (D) 1920 was well after the war's conclusion and these military victories.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006015](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/006015)*

**Q395. The unit of current is**

- **A.** ohm
- **B.** watt
- **C.** ampere  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** The ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current, named after physicist André-Marie Ampère, and measures the flow of electric charge through a conductor. Why not the others: (A) ohm is the unit of electrical resistance, not current; (B) watt is the unit of power (energy per unit time), not current; (D) there is a correct answer among the given options, so 'none of the above' is incorrect.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/022001)*

- **B.** Circumnavigation of the earth by car  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Formula One racing
- **D.** Buggy-Jumping

**Explanation:** Circumnavigating of the earth by car. This trophy has been named after Saloo and Neena Choudhury, ace car rallyists from Calcutta. It's in recognition of the couple's achievement in 1989 when they passed through all six continents in 69 days and repeated the same feat in 1991 in 39 days.

- **A.** Two
- **B.** Three
- **C.** Four  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** Eight

- **B.** Martin Luther King
- **C.** Toni Morrison  ✅ **(correct answer)**

**Q403. How long are professional Golf Tour players allotted per shot?**

- **A.** 45 seconds  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** 25 seconds
- **C.** 1 minute
- **D.** 2 minutes

**Explanation:** Professional golf tour players are allotted 45 seconds per shot under the PGA Tour's pace-of-play regulations, which aim to keep tournament rounds moving at an efficient pace while maintaining competitive integrity. Why not the others: (B) 25 seconds is too brief for the deliberate, technical nature of professional golf shots; (C) 1 minute exceeds the standard time limit set by major tour authorities; (D) 2 minutes is double the actual allowance and would result in excessively long tournament rounds.

**Q405. India's first satellite is named after**

- **A.** Aryabhatta  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **B.** Bhaskara II
- **C.** Bhaskara I
- **D.** Albert Einstein

**Explanation:** India's first satellite, launched on April 19, 1975, was named Aryabhata after the renowned ancient Indian mathematician and astronomer who made significant contributions to mathematics and astronomy. This choice honored India's scientific heritage and intellectual traditions. Why not the others: (B) Bhaskara II was a later medieval mathematician, not the inspiration for the first satellite; (C) Bhaskara I was an earlier astronomer but was not selected for this honor; (D) Albert Einstein was a Western scientist and not relevant to India's choice of naming its pioneering satellite after an Indian scientific luminary.

- **B.** Rani Lakshmi

- **D.** Saraswati

Why not the others: (A) Golden Globe is a film and entertainment industry award, not relevant to Kiran Bedi's profile; (B) Rani Lakshmi does not exist as a major national or international award; (D) Saraswati is not an award conferred on individuals for distinguished service in the manner associated with Kiran Bedi's recognition.

**Q407. Eritrea, which became the 182nd member of the UN in 1993, is in the continent of**

- **A.** Asia
- **B.** Africa  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Europe
- **D.** Australia

**Explanation:** Eritrea is located on the eastern coast of Africa, specifically in the Horn of Africa region, bordering the Red Sea. It became an independent nation in 1993 after separating from Ethiopia and was admitted as the 182nd UN member state that same year. Why not the others: (A) Eritrea is not in Asia, though it is geographically close to the Arabian Peninsula across the Red Sea; (C) Eritrea is not in Europe, which is a separate continent to the north; (D) Eritrea is not in Australia, which is a continent in the Southern Hemisphere.

- **C.** 'Mirza Ghalib', Sohrab Modi

Why not the others: (B) Sir Syed Ahmed was a 19th-century social reformer, not a film producer; (C) 'Mirza Ghalib' was a later Sohrab Modi film and not India's first Technicolor film; (D) Munshi Premchand was a renowned literary figure who passed away in 1936, before the Technicolor era of Indian cinema began.

Subbulakshmi was awarded it in 1998, not 1999; (D) Atal Behari Vajpayee received it in 2015, not 1999.

- **A.** Sathish Dawan
- **B.** C. Subramanian
- **C.** Grameen Bank of Bangladesh  ✅ **(correct answer)**

He was a Bengali polymath whose contributions to literature and culture made him an iconic figure across Asia and the world. Why not the others: (A) C. V. Raman won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930, making him the first Asian-Indian to win it, but this was after Tagore; (B) Rajiv Gandhi was an Indian Prime Minister but never won a Nobel Prize; (D) Mother Teresa won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979, which was decades after Tagore's 1913 win.

Why not the others: (A) Martin Luther King received the Nobel Peace Prize but not the first Nehru Award; (B) Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, though a prominent freedom fighter, was not the first recipient of this specific award; (C) Mother Teresa received the Nehru Award later but was not the first recipient.

**Q422. The reaction which converts sugar solution into alcohol is an example of**

- **A.** saponification
- **B.** hydrogenation
- **C.** fermentation  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** hydrolysis

**Explanation:** Fermentation is the biological process where microorganisms like yeast convert sugars into alcohol and carbon dioxide in the absence of oxygen, which is exactly what happens when sugar solution is converted into alcohol. Why not the others: (A) saponification is the chemical process of making soap from fats and oils, not related to alcohol production; (B) hydrogenation is the addition of hydrogen to unsaturated compounds to make them saturated, not a fermentation process; (D) hydrolysis is the breakdown of compounds by water molecules, not the biological conversion of sugar to alcohol.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/021001)*

---

**Q423. When was Amateur Athletics Federation of India established?**

- **A.** 1936
- **B.** 1946  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** 1956
- **D.** 1966

**Explanation:** Amateur Athletic Federation of India (AAFI) was formed in 1946 at the initiative of Maharaja Yadvindra Singh, the then President of Indian Olympic Association with Prof. G.D. Sondhi as its first President.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012002](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/sports/012002)*

## Current Affairs — Standard 9

**Q424. India has largest deposits of ____ in the world.**

- **A.** gold
- **B.** copper
- **C.** mica  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** None of the above

**Explanation:** The British Geological Survey reports that as of 2005, Kodarma district in Jharkhand state in India had the largest deposits of mica in the world.

**Q425. The Indian Air Force celebrated its Golden Jubilee in**

- **A.** 1962
- **B.** 1972
- **C.** 1982  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **D.** 1992

**Explanation:** The Indian Air Force was officially established on 8th October 1932, and on 1st April 1954, Air Marshal Subroto Mukherjee, one of the founding members of the Air Force took over as the first Indian Chief of Air Staff.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/020001)*

**Q426. Garampani sanctuary is located at**

- **A.** Junagarh, Gujarat
- **B.** Diphu, Assam  ✅ **(correct answer)**
- **C.** Kohima, Nagaland
- **D.** Gangtok, Sikkim

**Explanation:** Garampani sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Diphu, Assam, known primarily for its conservation of the Indian bison (gaur) and other wildlife species in the northeastern region. Why not the others: (A) Junagarh in Gujarat is known for the Gir Forest National Park and lion sanctuary, not Garampani; (C) Kohima in Nagaland does not house Garampani sanctuary; (D) Gangtok in Sikkim is associated with different protected areas and sanctuaries in that state.

*Source: [https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/](https://www.indiabix.com/general-knowledge/basic-general-knowledge/)*
